Name: gold-medal-onion-rings-recipe-with-italian-breadcrumbs
Ingredients: ['2 large yellow onion, sliced into ½-inch rings and separated', '½ cup all-purpose flour', '3 large eggs, beaten', '¾ cup milk', '2 cups panko breadcrumbs', 'Vegetable oil, for frying']
Seasoning: ['¾ cup grated Parmesan cheese', '1 Tbsp.  Italian seasoning', '1 tsp. garlic powder', '½  tsp. salt']
Procedure:
['
In a shallow bowl, add the flour. In a second bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs and milk. In a third bowl, combine the panko breadcrumbs, grated Parmesan, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, and salt.
', '
Dip each onion ring into the flour, shaking off excess. Transfer it into the egg mixture, coating fully, then press it into the panko-Parmesan mixture until well coated. Place finished rings on a plate and repeat with all remaining onions.
', '
In a saucepan, add 2 inches of vegetable oil and heat to 375°F. Once hot, carefully lower a few coated onion rings into the oil, frying for 2–3 minutes per side or until deep golden brown.
', '
In a saucepan, add 2 inches of vegetable oil and heat to 375°F. Once hot, carefully lower a few coated onion rings into the oil, frying for 2–3 minutes per side or until deep golden brown.
', '
Serve immediately with Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo Dressing/Spread as the dipping sauce.
\xa0
Find more gameday appetizer recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: bbq-ribs-recipe-with-chinese-bbq-sauce
Ingredients: ['1 rack St. Louis–style pork ribs (about 3 lbs)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Marinade: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '3 Tbsp. brown sugar', '1 Tbsp. salt', '3 Tbsp. ketchup', '3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Shaoxing wine', '1 Tbsp. pineapple juice', '1 tsp. five spice powder', '1/2 tsp. white pepper']
Procedure:
['
In a medium bowl, whisk together the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, brown sugar, salt, ketchup, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce, Shaoxing wine, pineapple juice, five spice powder, and white pepper. Set aside 3 Tbsp of this marinade in a small bowl for glazing later.
', '
Before marinating, check the bone side of the ribs and remove the silver skin membrane if it is still attached. Place the rack of ribs on a sheet pan and rub all remaining marinade over the ribs until fully coated. Cover and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight for maximum flavor.
', '
When ready to cook, preheat the oven to 375°F. Line a rimmed baking sheet with heavy-duty foil and set a metal rack on top. Place the ribs bone-side up on the rack. Pour 1 cup hot water into the foil-lined pan beneath the rack and roast for 30 minutes.
', '
Warm the reserved 3 Tbsp marinade with the honey in the microwave or a small saucepan until smooth. Carefully flip the ribs, add another thin layer of hot water to the pan if needed, and roast 30 minutes more. Then cover the entire pan tightly with foil and continue roasting 30–45 minutes (30 minutes for a firmer bite, 45 minutes for more tender ribs).
', '
Remove the foil, baste both sides of the ribs generously with the honey glaze, and roast for 10 minutes. Repeat basting and roast a final 10 minutes until glossy, lacquered, and deeply caramelized. Rest 10 minutes before slicing and serving. Enjoy!
\xa0
Get more Asian fusion BBQ recipes on our recipes page!


 


']

Name: cantonese-style-steamed-fish-recipe-with-gluten-free-soy-sauce
Ingredients: ['2 stalks scallions, julienned (2 inch lengths, cut in half)', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '1 small bunch cilantro, roughly chopped', '1 10 oz. fillet of delicate white fish (such as sea bass, grey sole or flounder)', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil']
Seasoning: ['1 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Gluten Free Soy Sauce', '⅛ tsp. salt', '⅛ tsp. sugar', '2 Tbsp. hot water']
Procedure:
['
Combine the Lee Kum Kee Gluten Free Soy Sauce, salt, sugar and hot water in a small bowl and mix until the sugar and salt are dissolved. Set aside.
', '
Prepare your steaming set-up and fill with 1-2 inches of water. Bring to a boil.
\xa0
Rinse your fish fillet and carefully lay it on a heat-proof plate that will fit into your wok or steaming setup. Carefully place it in the steamer and adjust the heat to medium. The water should be at a slow boil that generates a good amount of steam, but not so high that the water evaporates too quickly.
\xa0
Cover and steam for 7-10 minutes depending upon the size and thickness of your fish fillet. For thinner fillets (less than half an inch), cook for 4-5 minutes. Check for doneness using a butter knife. If it falls easily through the thickest part of the fillet to the bottom of the plate, the fish is done.
', '
Turn off the heat and carefully drain any liquid on the plate.
\xa0
On a separate burner, heat a wok or small saucepan to medium high heat and add 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil. Add the Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger, and fry for 1 minute. Add the white parts of the scallions and cook for 30 seconds. Then add half of the scallion greens and half of the cilantro and stir until the whole mixture is sizzling.
\xa0
Add the soy sauce mixture from step 1 to the pan or wok. Bring the mixture to a bubble and cook until the scallions and cilantro are just wilted, about 30 seconds.
', '
Pour this mixture over the fish, then top with the remaining cilantro and scallion greens. Heat the remaining tablespoon of oil until sizzling hot and pour over the aromatics and fish so they are sizzling as well. Serve immediately to get the best of the aromas!
\xa0
Looking for more gluten free Asian recipes? Try this gluten free hoisin meatballs recipe, which also uses Gluten Free Soy Sauce!
']

Name: mushroom-longevity-noodles-recipe-for-new-year
Ingredients: ['12 oz. Yi Mein Noodles ', 'Water, for boiling noodles ', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '½ lb. oyster mushrooms, cut into bite-size pieces', '1 cup fresh shiitake mushrooms, sliced', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '1 Tbsp. Shaoxing wine ', '8 stalks garlic chives (or green onions), cut into 1” pieces']
Seasoning: ['3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce ', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce ', '1 tsp. sugar', 'Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Chili Crisp Oil, for drizzling']
Procedure:
['
Boil noodles according to package instructions. Drain, rinse under cold water to stop cooking and remove excess starch, then drain thoroughly. Set aside. 
', '
In a small bowl, whisk together Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce, and sugar until combined. 
', '
Heat oil in a large pan or wok over high heat. Add oyster mushrooms and sear until golden, about 3 minutes. Add shiitake mushrooms and cook 1–2 minutes until slightly softened. Reduce heat to medium and stir in Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger. Cook for 1-2 minutes or until softened and aromatic.\xa0 Pour in Shaoxing wine and stir to deglaze the pan. 
', '
Add cooked noodles and toss a few times. Drizzle the sauce over noodles. Stir and cook until noodles are evenly coated and heated through. 
', '
Add garlic chives (or green onions) and stir 1–2 minutes until wilted. Transfer to a serving platter and garnish with a generous drizzle of Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Chili Crisp Oil. Serve immediately and enjoy.
\xa0
Get more recipes for the Year of the Horse for the festive occasion on our recipes page!
']

Name: beef-pho-dip-sandwich-recipe
Ingredients: ['--FOR THE PHO BROTH AU JUS--', '2 cups leftover pho broth ', '1 tsp fish sauce (optional, for extra umami) ', '1 star anise pod ', '1 small cinnamon stick ', '--FOR THE SANDWICH--', '2 small baguettes or french rolls, sliced and lightly toasted ', '½ lb cooked beef brisket, thinly sliced', '1 tsp neutral oil', '½ small white onion, thinly sliced ', '1 small bunch fresh cilantro leaves', '1 small bunch Thai basil leaves', '1 jalapeno, sliced ', 'Lime wedges, for serving ', '--FOR THE PARMESAN FRIES--', '1 lb frozen or fresh French fries', '1 tsp neutral oil ', 'Salt, to taste ', '¼ cup grated Parmesan cheese', '2 tbsp fresh parsley, finely chopped']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce, for drizzling', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce, for drizzling ']
Procedure:
['
Preheat the oven to 425°F. Add fries to a sheet pan and drizzle oil. Season with salt to taste. Bake for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway. Transfer to a bowl and toss with parmesan and parsley. Set aside and keep warm. 
', '
Pour pho broth into a pot and add star anise and cinnamon. Bring to a gentle simmer for 5–10 minutes to infuse flavors. Stir in fish sauce and taste for seasoning.\xa0
', '
Add brisket slices to the pot and heat until warmed and aromatic, about 2-3 minutes. Set aside and keep warm. 
', '
Layer warm brisket slices inside each baguette. Drizzle Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce and Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce on the meat. Add sliced onions, jalapeno, cilantro, and Thai basil on top.
', '
Pour warm pho au jus into small bowls alongside the sandwiches for dipping. Serve with parmesan french fries and lime wedges. Enjoy immediately.\xa0
\xa0
Discover more tasty and easy lunch ideas on our recipes page!
']

Name: orange-chicken-pizza-recipe
Ingredients: ['--FOR THE ORANGE CHICKEN--', '1 lb. chicken thighs, cut into bite-size pieces', '1 egg', '½ cup cornstarch', '½ cup all-purpose flour', 'Oil, for shallow frying', '--FOR THE PIZZA--', '1 pizza dough (12-inch)', '½ cup shredded mozzarella', '1 Tbsp. sliced green onions, for garnish ', '1 tsp. toasted sesame seeds, for garnish ']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 cup Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Sauce For Orange Chicken', '2 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey, for drizzling ']
Procedure:
['
Whisk the egg and Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce in a bowl. Add chicken pieces and coat well. In a separate bowl, mix cornstarch and flour. Dredge chicken thoroughly.
', '
Heat ½ inch of oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Fry chicken in batches for 4–5 minutes, until crispy and golden. Toss the hot chicken in the\xa0Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand\xa0Sauce For Orange Chicken and set aside. 
', '
Preheat the oven to 475°F. Roll out pizza dough to a 12-inch circle and place on a sheet pan. Lightly brush dough with olive oil. 
', '
Sprinkle mozzarella evenly over the base. Add the orange chicken pieces across the pizza.
', '
Bake for 10–12 minutes, until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbling. Let the pizza rest for 1–2 minutes. Add a generous drizzle of Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey for sweet heat. Garnish with sliced green onions and toasted sesame seeds. Slice and enjoy!
\xa0
Find more Asian fusion recipe ideas on our recipes page!
']

Name: kimchi-fried-rice-arancini-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '1 cup finely chopped kimchi', '2 cups cooked short-grain rice (day-old preferred)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 large egg, beaten (for binding)', '¼ cup shredded mozzarella', '½ cup all-purpose flour', '2 large eggs, beaten (for breading)', '1 cup panko breadcrumbs', 'Neutral oil, for frying', '1 tsp. black sesame seeds', '1 green onion, thinly sliced']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. kimchi juice', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce', '1 tsp. salt, divided', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo, plus more for dipping']
Procedure:
['
Heat butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped kimchi and sauté 2–3 minutes until lightly caramelized. Add the cooked rice and break up clumps with a spatula. Stir in Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce, and kimchi juice. Cook 1–2 minutes, stirring constantly, until coated and aromatic. Transfer to a bowl and let cool completely.
', '
Once the rice is fully cooled, mix in 1 beaten egg until evenly incorporated. This helps the arancini hold together when shaped and fried.
', '
Scoop about 2 Tbsp of the rice mixture and flatten into a disk in your hand. Place 1 Tbsp shredded mozzarella in the center, top with another 2 Tbsp rice, and shape into a tight ball, pressing with your palms so it’s compact.
', '
In one bowl, combine flour with ½ tsp salt. In a second bowl, add the beaten eggs. In a third bowl, combine panko with the remaining ½ tsp salt. Dredge each rice ball in flour, dip in egg, then roll in panko, pressing lightly so the crumbs adhere.
', '
Heat neutral oil to 350°F in a deep pan. Fry arancini in batches for 2–3 minutes or until deeply golden and crisp. Transfer to a wire rack and lightly season with salt. Top each arancini with Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo, then garnish with black sesame seeds and sliced green onion. Serve immediately with extra Sriracha Mayo for dipping.
\xa0
Get more gameday appetizer recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: crab-rangoon-pull-apart-bread-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 round loaf of bread (sourdough, boule, or rustic white bread works best)', '2–3 Tbsp. unsalted butter, melted', '8 oz. cream cheese, softened', '½ cup crab meat (real or imitation), chopped  ', '1 tsp. garlic powder', '1 Tbsp. green onions, thinly sliced, plus more for garnish ', '½ cup shredded mozzarella cheese ', '¼ cup grated parmesan cheese, plus more for garnish']
Seasoning: ['2–3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo', 'Salt and pepper, to taste', '2 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey, for drizzling  ']
Procedure:
['
Preheat oven to 350°F. Using a serrated knife, cut the bread into a grid pattern: Slice vertically and horizontally about ¾ of the way down into the loaf, forming 1–2 inch squares or diamonds.
', '
Make the Filling: In a medium bowl, combine cream cheese, crab meat, Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo, garlic powder, and green onions. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
', '
Spoon the crab and cheese mixture into the crevices of the bread. Use a small spoon or piping bag to get filling deep into the cuts.
', '
Drizzle melted butter over the top of the loaf so it seeps into the crevices. Tuck mozzarella cheese into cuts then finish with parmesan. 
', '
Bake for 15–20 minutes, until the filling is hot and the cheese is melted. Optional: broil 1–2 minutes at the end to brown the top and create a golden, bubbly finish. Garnish with green onions and freshly grated parmesan. Drizzle Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey generously over the loaf. Serve immediately while hot and cheesy.
\xa0
Want to try Chili Crisp Hot Honey on this recipe? Find it near you on our product page along with more recipe ideas featuring the sweet and spicy sauce!
']

Name: air-fryer-hot-honey-chicken-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 lb. thinly sliced chicken breasts ', '½ cup all-purpose flour', '1 large egg, lightly beaten', '2 cups crushed cornflake cereal', 'Oil, for spraying the chicken + air fryer basket', 'Minced fresh parsley, for garnish ', 'Store-bought ranch dressing, for dipping (optional) ']
Seasoning: ['1 ½ tsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. black pepper', '1 tsp. smoked paprika', '½ tsp. garlic powder']
Marinade: ['3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha, plus more for drizzling', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, mix together Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce and Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce until combined. Add the chicken and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for 20-30 minutes. Remove chicken and pat lightly with a paper towel to remove excess marinade.\xa0
', '
Place flour in a shallow bowl. Beat egg in a separate shallow bowl. In a third bowl, mix crushed cornflakes with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, and garlic powder.
', '
Dredge each chicken piece in flour, shaking off excess. Dip in egg, letting excess drip off. Press into cornflake crumbs, coating all sides.\xa0
', '
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F. Spray the basket lightly with oil. Arrange chicken in a single layer. Lightly spray tops with oil. Air fry 8–10 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden brown and internal temp reaches 165°F.
', '
Drizzle additional Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha generously over the chicken. Garnish with minced parsley if desired and enjoy with ranch.\xa0
\xa0
Check out more chicken recipes for meal prep on our recipes page!
']

Name: tingly-shredded-chicken
Ingredients: ['2 slices ginger', '1 stalk scallion (sectioned)', '2 pcs chicken thighs', '2.5 oz shredded cucumber', 'cilantro, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Green Sichuan Peppercorn Oil', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Bouillon Powder Flavored with Chicken', '1/2 tsp. salt', '1/2 tsp. sugar']
Procedure:
['
Add ginger slices, scallions, and water into the pot and bring it to a boil. Add chicken thighs over low heat until fully cooked. Take out the chicken thighs and put it in cold water.
', '
Pat the chicken thighs dry. Tear the chicken thighs into shreds by hands.
', '
Mix shredded chicken, shredded cucumber, and seasoning well. Add cilantro as garnish and serve.
']

Name: chinese-style-red-braised-chicken-recipe---hong-shao-ji
Ingredients: ['4 bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs', 'Salt, to taste ', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic ', '2–3 green onions, cut into 2-inch pieces', '1–2 star anise', '1 cinnamon stick', '1 cup chicken stock (or 1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon Powder dissolved in 1 cup of boiling water)', 'Cooked white rice, for serving ', 'Thinly sliced green onions, for garnish ']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce ', '¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Shaoxing wine (or dry sherry)', '1–2 Tbsp. (0.5-1 oz) rock sugar or granulated sugar']
Procedure:
['
Pat the chicken thighs dry and lightly season with salt.\xa0Heat the vegetable oil in a deep skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chicken skin-side down and sear 3–4 minutes per side until golden. Remove and set aside.
', '
In the same pan, add the Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, scallions, star anise, and cinnamon stick. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.\xa0Add the Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce, Shaoxing wine, and sugar. Stir to dissolve the sugar and coat the aromatics.
', '
Return chicken to the pan, skin-side up. Add your preferred chicken stock or Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon Powder and hot water — it should come about halfway up the thighs. If using Chicken Bouillon Powder, add 1 tsp for every cup of hot water added. 
', '
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer. Cover and cook for 25–30 minutes.\xa0Remove the lid and increase heat to medium. Simmer for another 4–6 minutes until the liquid thickens into a glossy sauce.
', '
Spoon the sauce over the chicken and garnish with sliced green onions. Enjoy with steamed rice.
\xa0
Get more Lunar New Year recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: soy-sauce-marinated-ramen-eggs-recipe
Ingredients: ['6 large eggs, at room temperature', 'Water, for boiling the eggs', 'Large ice water bath for cooling the eggs (about 6-8 cups of water with 2 cups of ice)']
Marinade: ['½ cup Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 ½ cups water', '½ cup mirin', '¼ cup granulated sugar', '3-4 cloves garlic, smashed ', '1 2-inch section whole ginger, crushed']
Procedure:
['
Get your eggs out of the refrigerator and let rest until they come to room temperature. This should happen at the same time it takes for your water to boil on the stove. You can also prepare the ice bath at this time by mixing together a 4:1 ratio of water to ice in a large bowl. 
', '
Once the water on the stove is at a gentle boil, carefully lay the eggs in so that they don’t crack. Cook for 6 ½ -9 minutes, depending on how jammy you want your eggs. 6 minutes is the minimum time for the egg whites to set properly. Anything above 10 minutes will get into hard boiled territory. 
', '
Immediately move the eggs from the boiling water into your ice bath with a slotted spoon to stop the cooking process. Let them go completely cold in the ice bath.
', '
While the eggs cool, make your marinade. Bash the whole ginger and garlic so it is crushed to release the flavors. Add to a small saucepan with 1 ½ cups water, ½ cup Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, ½ cup mirin, and ¼ cup sugar. Bring to a boil then immediately lower and simmer for 2-3 minutes, ensuring the sugar completely dissolves. Turn off the heat and let sit to the side to cool.
', '
Once your eggs are completely chilled, tap them lightly on a flat surface to help easily peel the eggs. Transfer peeled eggs to an airtight container and pour the marinade over the top. Cover and let marinade overnight, at least 12 hours and up to 24 hours. 
', '
When ready to enjoy the eggs, they’re best served cut in half on top of a bowl of ramen noodles or rice. Enjoy, and check out more Asian egg recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: air-fryer-xo-fish-balls-in-honey
Ingredients: ['10 oz. fish paste']
Seasoning: ['3 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce (oil removed)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Procedure:
['
Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.
', '
Take a small portion (around 0.8 oz.) and knead as a ball. Repeat the step until all the mixture are done.
', '
Put the fish balls in the air-fryer. Air-fry them at 392°F for 10 minutes.
', '
Add in honey and toss well. Put it back to air-fryer again. Air-fry for 3 minutes at 356°F.
', '
Tips:
1. When cooking at home, wet your hands with water before you knead. It helps to avoid sticking your hands from the fish paste.

2. Prepare a plate with plastic wrap and brush a layer of oil. Then put the fish balls\xa0in step 2 on the plate. It helps to avoid sticking the plate from the fish balls.
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. baking soda    ', '1 tsp. cornstarch    ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce      ', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['2 oz. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. Chinese rice wine (or white wine)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon ', '1/3 cup water', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
PREPARE THE BROCCOLI. Blanch the broccoli in hot salted water for about 2 minutes or until slightly tender and dark green. Shock it in ice water and drain thoroughly.\xa0\xa0
', '
MARINATE THE BEEF. In a small bowl, whisk together the baking soda, cornstarch, Lee Kum Kee Cooking Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Pour the mixed sauces over your thinly sliced beef flank steak and let it sit for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
\xa0
', '
CREATE THE SAUCE MIX. In a separate small bowl, stir together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Chinese rice wine, Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon, water, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
', '
STIR-FRY. Heat a wok or a large skillet on high heat and add the oil. When a wisp of white smoke appears, toss the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and marinated beef into the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, continually moving the beef slices so they are brown on all sides, but still rare. Once the beef is seared, add the broccoli and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the sauce and keep it all moving.
', '
FINISH AND SERVE. Don’t be afraid to scrape any bits off the bottom of the pan before it starts to burn. Once the sauce has turned into a nice thick glaze, serve immediately with garnishes (optional). Enjoy!
']

Name: beef-chow-fun-noodles-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 lb. sirloin or flank steak, cut into bite sized pieces', '2 Tbsp. neutral oil for cooking', '1 ½ lbs. wide flat rice noodles, fresh or dried + pre-soaked in warm water', '½ cup white onion, thinly sliced', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '2 cups bean sprouts, washed', '4 stalks green onion, trimmed, washed, and cut into thirds', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Marinade: ['1/2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce', '½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 tsp baking powder', '1 Tbsp. Cornstarch', '½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee White Pepper', '1 Tbsp. neutral oil']
Sauce Mix: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce', '2 Tbsp. granulated sugar', '2 tsp. neutral oil']
Procedure:
['
Make the beef marinade by combining ½ Tbsp. each of Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce and Premium Dark Soy Sauce in a large bowl. Add 1 tsp. baking powder, 1 Tbsp. cornstarch, ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee White Pepper Powder, and 1 Tbsp. neutral flavored oil. Combine well, then add beef to the marinade and mix well so every piece is fully coated. Set aside for 20 minutes.
', '
While the beef marinades, prep your veggies. Thinly slice the white onion, wash and trim bean sprouts and green onion stalks, and cut the green onions into thirds. 
', '
Pre-soak chow fun noodles (wide flat rice noodles) to rehydrate. For fresh noodles, blanch for 1 minute in hot water, rinse in cold water to stop the cooking, then toss with a touch of oil to prevent sticking. For dried noodles, soaked for at least 10 minutes in warm or hot water until pliable. 
', '
Make the sauce mix by combining 1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, 2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce, 1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce, 2 Tbsp. granulated sugar, and 2 tsp. neutral cooking oil in a small bowl. Set aside for cooking.
', '
Heat a wok or large frying pan to high heat and add neutral oil to coat the bottom of the pan. Add the marinated beef and sear for 1 minute on each side, then remove from the heat. 
', '
Add the white onions and sauté for 30 seconds, then follow with the green onions and Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic. Stir fry for another minute, then add the beef back in along with your rice noodles. Stir or toss to combine, then add your sauce mix and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring or tossing gently to coat all the noodles without breaking them. Turn off the heat, toss through 2 tsp. of Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil to finish, and serve.
\xa0
Enjoy, and check out more easy 30-minute meals on our recipes page!\xa0 
']

Name: hoisin-crispy-rice-burger-recipe
Ingredients: ['2 cups cooked sushi rice (short-grain rice)', '2 tsp. neutral oil, for cooking', '1/2 lb. ground beef (80/20 preferred)', '4 thin slices cheddar or American cheese ', '4-6 dill pickle slices ', '2-4 crisp lettuce leaves (butter or romaine lettuce)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional)']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar', '1 tsp. sugar', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce ', 'Salt and pepper to taste ', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, mix the cooked sushi rice with Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar and sugar until well combined. Divide the rice into 4 equal portions. Using your hands, shape each portion into a round, flat patty (about the size of a burger bun).\xa0
', '
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and lightly coat with neutral oil. Fry the rice patties for 3-4 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy. Remove from the pan and set aside.
', '
In a bowl, gently mix ground beef with\xa0Lee Kum\xa0Kee\xa0Hoisin Sauce. Form into 4 loose balls about 2 oz. each and set aside.
', '
Heat a cast iron skillet or griddle over medium-high heat. Add beef balls and smash each firmly with a spatula. Season with salt and pepper. Cook for 2–3 minutes until edges are crispy, then flip and top with cheese. Cook 1 more minute until melted. Remove from heat
', '
Spread Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo on the bottom rice bun. Add pickles then stack two smashed patties with melted cheese. Top with lettuce leaves and drizzle additional Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo onto the burger patties. Cap with the top bun and enjoy!
\xa0
Get more hoisin recipe ideas on our recipes page!
']

Name: peri-peri-chicken-and-jollof-rice-bowls-recipe
Ingredients: ['4 chicken thighs or leg quarters', '2 cups long-grain rice ', '1 medium onion, finely diced', '1 red bell pepper ', '1 can (6 oz.) tomato paste', '1–2 cups chicken stock (enough to just cover rice)', '1–2 Tbsp. neutral oil', '1/2 cup Peri Peri Sauce (see Sauce Mix), for serving', 'Plantain chips, for serving', 'Coleslaw, for serving', 'Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish ']
Seasoning: ['Jollof Rice Seasoning:', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce ', '2 tsp. smoked paprika', '1 tsp. curry powder', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '1 tsp. dried thyme', '1 bay leaf', 'Salt, to taste']
Marinade: ['1 1/2 cups Peri-Peri Sauce (see Sauce Mix)', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 Tbsp. neutral oil', 'Salt, to taste']
Sauce Mix: ['Peri-Peri Sauce: ', "2 bird's eye chili peppers", '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce ', '1 red bell pepper, seeded and chopped', '2 Roma tomatoes, halved ', '½ yellow onion, chopped ', '2 Tbsp. lemon juice', '1 Tbsp. dried oregano', '1 tsp. smoked paprika ', '1 Tbsp. balsamic vinegar', '3 Tbsp. extra virgin olive oil ', 'Salt and pepper, to taste ']
Procedure:
['
Make the Peri Peri Sauce: Add bird’s eye chili, Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce, bell pepper, tomatoes, onion, lemon juice, oregano, paprika and balsamic vinegar to a food processor. Blend until smooth, adding in olive oil 1 Tbsp at a time until sauce is combined and slightly thickened. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
\xa0
This should make about 2 cups of Peri Peri Sauce to use for the marinade and for serving.
', '
Make the Peri-Peri Chicken: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Pat the chicken dry with paper towels.
In a bowl, toss chicken with Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce until fully coated. Add 1 ½ cups\xa0of Peri-Peri sauce and oil to the chicken and mix to combine. 
Arrange chicken on a baking sheet and roast for 30–35 minutes, flipping halfway through, until cooked through (internal temperature reaches 165°F) and slightly charred at the edges.
*Note: You can also grill the chicken for extra charred flavor! Marinade the chicken for at least 10 minutes while heating up the grill, then cook for about 10 minutes on each side until the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
', '
Make the Jollof Rice Tomato Base: Char red bell pepper directly on the stovetop over medium-high heat until the skin is blackened and blistered. Allow to cool, then peel and chop.
Add the chopped bell pepper and tomato paste to a blender or food processor. Blend until smooth and set aside.
', '
Make the Jollof Rice: Heat oil in a deep saucepan over medium heat. Sauté diced onion for 2–3 minutes until soft and fragrant.
Add Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce, smoked paprika, curry powder, Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger, thyme, bay leaf, and blended tomato mixture. Stir to combine and simmer for 5 minutes to build flavor.
Stir in rice and just enough chicken stock to cover the grains. Mix gently once.
Cover tightly, reduce heat to low, and cook for 25–30 minutes until rice is tender and fluffy. Taste and adjust salt if needed. 
', '
Serve chicken atop or alongside the jollof rice. Lightly warm the reserved Peri-Peri sauce and spoon over the chicken. 
Garnish with chopped cilantro and enjoy with fried plantain chips and coleslaw.
Enjoy, and discover more chicken dinner recipe ideas on our recipes page!
']

Name: holiday-fusion-red-chile-pork-tamales-recipe
Ingredients: ['-- FOR THE PORK FILLING --', '2½ lbs. pork shoulder, cut into large chunks', '1 small onion, halved', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. cumin', '2 dried guajillo chiles, stemmed, seeded', '2 dried ancho chiles, stemmed, seeded', '2 cups hot water (for soaking chiles)', '1 Tbsp. tomato paste', '1 tsp. Mexican oregano', '1 tsp. smoked paprika', '1 Tbsp. oil (for sautéing)', '-- FOR THE MASA DOUGH --', '4 cups masa harina', '2¾ cups warm pork broth (from cooking the pork)', '1½ tsp baking powder', '1½ tsp kosher salt', '1½ cups lard or neutral oil', '20–25 dried corn husks', 'Steamer pot or tamalera']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce']
Procedure:
['
In a large pot, add pork, onion, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, salt, and cumin. Cover with water and simmer 1½ hours until pork is tender. Reserve broth. Shred the pork and set aside.
\xa0
Once the pork is shredded and cooling, soak the corn husks in warm water for 30 minutes until pliable. In a separate bowl, soak the guajillo and ancho chiles for about 20 minutes.
', '
Blend soaked chiles with Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce, tomato paste, oregano, paprika, and about ¾ cup soaking liquid until smooth. Sauté sauce in a pan with oil, then add shredded pork and about 1 cup reserved pork broth. Simmer 10–15 minutes until thick.
', '
Mix masa harina, baking powder, and salt. Add warm pork broth and mix until combined. Add lard and beat with hands or stand mixer until fluffy and soft but not sticky.
', '
Spread 2–3 tbsp masa on the wide part of each soaked + pliable husk. Spoon 2 tbsp pork filling in center. Fold the sides in, then the bottom up. Repeat for each tamale - this should make 20-25 tamales in total.
', "
Place tamales upright in steamer with open ends up. Steam covered for 1½–2 hours, refilling water as needed. They're done when the masa pulls away clean from husk.
\xa0
For more spicy pork recipes, check out our recipes page!
"]

Name: crispy-fried-shrimp-balls---dim-sum-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 lb. raw shrimp, peeled, deveined, finely chopped into a paste', '1½ tbsp cornstarch', 'Neutral oil for frying', '10 sheets spring roll wrappers, cut into ¼‑inch strips', 'Green onions, chopped, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '½ tsp. white pepper', '½ tsp. kosher salt', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 tsp. sugar', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil ', 'Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, for drizzling']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, combine shrimp paste, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, white pepper, salt, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce, sugar, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, and cornstarch. Mix in one direction until it binds. Chill in freezer 20‑30 minutes to firm up.
', '
Cut spring roll wrappers into thin ¼‑inch strips and lay them out.\xa0Wet your hands and shape the shrimp mixture into heaping tablespoon balls. Roll each ball in the wrapper strips so it is fully coated.
\xa0
*Pro Tip: for a wider noodle, cut into 1/2-inch strips.*
', '
Heat neutral oil to 350°F (175°C) in a deep pot or wok. Fry the shrimp balls in batches for about 4‑5 minutes until golden brown and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
', "
Arrange on a serving plate drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil. Garnish with chopped green onions and serve hot.
\xa0
We've got plenty of other Chinese recipes if you're wanting to learn how to make them!
"]

Name: baked-manapua-recipe---hawaiian-style-char-siu-buns
Ingredients: ['-- FOR THE DOUGH --', '3 cups all-purpose flour', '2¼ tsp. active dry yeast (1 packet)', '3 Tbsp. sugar', '½ tsp. salt', '¾ cup warm milk (110°F)', '¼ cup warm water', '3 Tbsp. neutral oil', '-- FOR THE PORK --', '1 lb. pork shoulder or pork butt, cut into long 1½-inch thick strips', 'Marinade Ingredients (see below)', '2 drops red food coloring (optional for traditional color)', '-- FOR THE EGG WASH --', '1 egg yolk', '1 Tbsp. milk']
Marinade: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. honey', '1 Tbsp. Shaoxing wine (or dry sherry)', '1½ tsp. Chinese five spice powder', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, mix Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce, Panda Brand Oyster Sauce, Premium Soy Sauce, honey, Shaoxing wine, five spice powder, Pure Sesame Oil, Minced Garlic, and food coloring (if using). Coat the pork evenly and marinate in the fridge for at least 1 hour or overnight.
', '
Make the dough while the char siu marinates. If marinating overnight, do this step just before you begin roasting in Step 3.
\xa0
In a large bowl, combine warm milk, water, yeast, and sugar. Let sit 5–10 minutes until frothy. Add oil, salt, and flour. Knead 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour or until doubled in size.
', '
Preheat oven to 400°F. Place marinated pork on a foil-lined rack over a baking tray. Roast 20 minutes, flip, baste with leftover marinade, and roast another 10–15 minutes until glossy and caramelized. Let rest 10 minutes, then finely dice.
', '
Pour any leftover marinade and pan drippings into a small saucepan. Simmer over medium heat until thickened slightly into a glaze. Stir in diced char siu pork and toss until coated and saucy. Set aside to cool.
', '
Punch down dough and divide into 10 pieces. Roll each into a ball, then flatten into a circle. Place a spoonful of saucy char siu filling in the center. Gather edges and pinch to seal, placing buns seam-side down on a parchment-lined tray. Proof 30 minutes.\xa0
', '
Preheat oven to 350°F, brush buns with egg wash, and bake 18–20 minutes until golden and shiny. These are best enjoyed warm, fresh from the oven!
\xa0
For more tasty pork recipes, follow our recipes page!
']

Name: mini-yorkshire-pudding-with-roast-beef-recipe
Ingredients: ['3 large eggs', '1 cup whole milk', '1 cup all-purpose flour', '½ tsp. salt', 'Oil (for muffin tin)', '½ lb. beef tenderloin or top sirloin', '1 Tbsp. olive oil', 'Prepared creamy horseradish sauce', 'Chopped parsley, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['1 tsp. garlic powder', '1 tsp. black pepper', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', 'Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, for drizzling']
Procedure:
['
Preheat oven to 450°F. Whisk eggs, milk, flour, and salt until smooth. Let batter rest 30 minutes at room temp. Add ½ tsp oil to each well of a muffin tin. Place tin in oven for 5 minutes until oil is very hot.
', '
Carefully pour batter evenly into each well. Bake 18–20 minutes without opening the oven, until puffed and golden. Remove and let cool slightly.
', '
While puddings bake, rub beef with olive oil, garlic powder, and pepper. Sear in a hot pan on all sides, about 2–3 minutes per side.
', '
Brush seared beef with\xa0Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce and roast at 400°F for 10–15 minutes (or until desired doneness). Rest and thinly slice.
', '
To assemble, top Yorkshire puddings with roast beef slices, top with horseradish cream, drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, and sprinkle with parsley.
\xa0
Check out more holiday appetizer ideas on our recipes page!
']

Name: holiday-grilled-cheese-and-tomato-soup-shooters-recipe
Ingredients: ['-- FOR THE SOUP --', '1 Tbsp. olive oil', '1 small yellow onion, diced', '1 (28 oz.) can crushed tomatoes', '1 cup vegetable broth', '1 Tbsp. tomato paste', '1 tsp. sugar', 'Salt and pepper, to taste', '2 Tbsp. heavy cream ', '-- FOR THE GRILLED CHEESE --', '6 slices sandwich bread', '1½ cups shredded mozzarella or cheddar cheese', 'Holiday-shaped cookie cutters (gingerbread man, candy cane, tree, etc.)', 'Rosemary sprigs, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce', '3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo']
Procedure:
['
In a pot over medium heat, add olive oil and sauté onion until translucent, about 5 minutes. Add Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce and cook 1 minute more.
', '
Stir in crushed tomatoes, broth, tomato paste, and sugar. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer uncovered for 15–20 minutes until slightly thickened. Use an immersion blender to purée the soup until smooth. Stir in heavy cream. Keep warm.
', '
\xa0While soup simmers, lay out 3 slices of bread, top each with shredded cheese, and close with another slice. Use cookie cutters to press shapes from each sandwich.
', '
Spread Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo on the outside of each shape. Toast in a nonstick skillet over medium heat until golden brown and melty, about 2–3 minutes per side.
', '
Pour soup into small shooter glasses or ramekins. Top each with a festive grilled cheese shape, garnish with rosemary sprigs and serve immediately.
\xa0
Find more ideas for holiday appetizers on our recipes page!
']

Name: easy-spicy-hoisin-noodles-recipe
Ingredients: ['10 oz. wide rice noodles (fresh or dried)', 'Fresh cilantro and green onions, chopped, for garnish']
Sauce Mix: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 tsp. sugar', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil (plus more for drizzling)', '2 Tbsp. neutral oil (like avocado or canola)', '1 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '1–2 Tbsp. noodle cooking water (if needed)']
Procedure:
['
If using dried noodles, cook according to package directions until just tender. Drain and rinse with cold water. If using fresh noodles, separate them and set aside.
', '
In a small bowl, whisk together Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, sugar, vinegar, and Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil. Set aside.
', '
Heat oil in a large pan or wok over medium heat. Add Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
', '
Add the noodles and stir-fry for 1–2 minutes. Pour in the sauce and toss to evenly coat, adding a splash of noodle water if needed to loosen.
', '
Stir-fry for another 1–2 minutes until noodles are glossy and fully coated. Remove from heat. Garnish with cilantro and green onions and an extra drizzle of Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil before serving.
\xa0
Get more ideas for simple 30-minute dinners on our recipes page!
']

Name: lu-rou-fan-recipe---taiwanese-style-braised-pork-rice
Ingredients: ['-- FOR THE BRAISED PORK -- ', '1 ½ lbs. pork belly, skin-on, cut into ¼-inch cubes', '1 Tbsp. neutral oil', '-- FOR THE SOY BRAISED EGGS -- ', '4 boiled eggs, peeled', '-- FOR ASSEMBLY -- ', '4 cups Steamed jasmine rice', '4 cups Blanched baby bok choy (about 8 pieces)', '1 cup Pickled mustard greens', '2 stalks chopped scallions, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['-- FOR THE BRAISED PORK -- ', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '2 Tbsp. shallots, finely chopped', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Shaoxing wine', '1 Tbsp. rice vinegar', '1 tsp. five spice powder', '1 ½ cups water', '1 Tbsp. crushed rock sugar, or brown sugar']
Sauce Mix: ['-- FOR THE SOY BRAISED EGGS -- ', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce', '½ cup water', '½ tsp. sugar', '2 whole star anise', '1 small piece dried tangerine or orange peel (optional)']
Procedure:
['
Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat. Add Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and shallots; cook until softened and lightly golden. Stir in diced pork belly and cook 5–7 minutes, until fat begins to render and pork is browned on all sides.
', '
Stir in Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, Premium Dark Soy Sauce, Premium Oyster Sauce, Shaoxing wine, rice vinegar, five spice powder, rock sugar, and water. Add star anise and tangerine/orange peel if using.
', '
Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Simmer uncovered for 60–75 minutes, stirring occasionally, until pork is tender and sauce is glossy and thickened.
', '
While pork simmers, place Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, Premium Dark Soy Sauce, water, and sugar in a small saucepan. Add peeled eggs and simmer 15–20 minutes, turning occasionally. Remove from heat and let eggs steep in the liquid until serving.
', "
To assemble, spoon braised pork over steamed jasmine rice. Halve soy braised eggs and arrange on top with blanched bok choy and pickled mustard greens. Garnish with scallions and drizzle extra sauce over the bowl.
\xa0
There's a lot of delicious Asian and Chinese pork recipes out there - discover more on our recipes page!
"]

Name: creamy-swedish-meatball-pasta-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 lb. ground beef', '1/4 cup grated onion', '1/2 cup panko breadcrumbs', '1 egg', '1 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '12 oz. shell pasta (or pasta of choice), cooked to package instructions', 'Fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['1/4 tsp. ground allspice', '1/4 tsp. ground nutmeg', '1/2 tsp. kosher salt', '1/4 tsp. black pepper', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce']
Sauce Mix: ['2 Tbsp unsalted butter', '2 Tbsp flour', '2 cups beef broth', '1/2 cup sour cream (room temperature)', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', 'Salt and pepper, to taste']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, combine ground beef, grated onion, panko, egg, allspice, nutmeg, salt, black pepper, and 1 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce. Mix just until combined and roll into 1-inch meatballs.
', '
Heat 1 Tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the meatballs in batches and sear until browned and cooked through. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
', '
In the same skillet, melt 2 Tbsp butter. Whisk in flour and cook for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in beef broth until smooth and thickened. Whisk in the remaining 1 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce and season with salt and pepper.
', '
Remove the skillet from heat. In a small bowl, temper the room-temperature sour cream by whisking in a ladle of the hot sauce until smooth. Slowly whisk the tempered sour cream back into the skillet until the sauce is creamy and combined.
', '
Return the meatballs to the sauce and coat. Spoon the mixture over the cooked pasta, garnish with parsley, and serve hot.
\xa0
Discover more dishes that show how to cook with oyster sauce on our recipes page!
']

Name: spicy-savory-smores-recipe---holiday-appetizers
Ingredients: ['12 sturdy savory crackers', '6 slices brie (cut into thick 1½–2 inch squares, rind on)', '6 slices prosciutto']
Seasoning: ['Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce, for drizzling']
Procedure:
['
Place brie slices on a heat-safe surface. Use a kitchen torch to gently blister and melt the tops until golden, bubbly, and caramelized. Set aside.
', '
Lay out 6 crackers. Top each with a folded slice of prosciutto. Drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce. Gently place a torched brie slice on top of each.
', '
Top with a second cracker to sandwich.\xa0Enjoy while the brie is still warm and melty for the ultimate sweet-spicy-salty bite.
\xa0
Check out more holiday appetizer recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: air-fryer-xo-fish-balls-in-honey
Ingredients: ['10 oz. fish paste']
Seasoning: ['3 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce (oil removed)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Procedure:
['
Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.
', '
Take a small portion (around 0.8 oz.) and knead as a ball. Repeat the step until all the mixture are done.
', '
Put the fish balls in the air-fryer. Air-fry them at 392°F for 10 minutes.
', '
Add in honey and toss well. Put it back to air-fryer again. Air-fry for 3 minutes at 356°F.
', '
Tips:
1. When cooking at home, wet your hands with water before you knead. It helps to avoid sticking your hands from the fish paste.

2. Prepare a plate with plastic wrap and brush a layer of oil. Then put the fish balls\xa0in step 2 on the plate. It helps to avoid sticking the plate from the fish balls.
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. baking soda    ', '1 tsp. cornstarch    ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce      ', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['2 oz. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. Chinese rice wine (or white wine)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon ', '1/3 cup water', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
PREPARE THE BROCCOLI. Blanch the broccoli in hot salted water for about 2 minutes or until slightly tender and dark green. Shock it in ice water and drain thoroughly.\xa0\xa0
', '
MARINATE THE BEEF. In a small bowl, whisk together the baking soda, cornstarch, Lee Kum Kee Cooking Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Pour the mixed sauces over your thinly sliced beef flank steak and let it sit for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
\xa0
', '
CREATE THE SAUCE MIX. In a separate small bowl, stir together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Chinese rice wine, Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon, water, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
', '
STIR-FRY. Heat a wok or a large skillet on high heat and add the oil. When a wisp of white smoke appears, toss the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and marinated beef into the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, continually moving the beef slices so they are brown on all sides, but still rare. Once the beef is seared, add the broccoli and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the sauce and keep it all moving.
', '
FINISH AND SERVE. Don’t be afraid to scrape any bits off the bottom of the pan before it starts to burn. Once the sauce has turned into a nice thick glaze, serve immediately with garnishes (optional). Enjoy!
']

Name: leftover-thanksgiving-sliders-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 package sweet Hawaiian rolls (12-count)', '¾ cup leftover cranberry sauce', '2 cups shredded cooked turkey', '1 ½ cups leftover stuffing', '3 Tbsp. melted butter', '1 tsp. garlic powder', '1 tsp. dried parsley']
Seasoning: ['¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce']
Procedure:
['
Preheat oven to 350°F. Slice the entire sheet of rolls in half horizontally, keeping tops and bottoms intact (Do not separate into individual sliders). 
', '
Place bottom halves in a greased baking dish. Spread cranberry sauce over the bottom, layer with turkey tossed in Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce, then pile on the stuffing. Top with the roll tops.
', '
Mix melted butter with garlic powder and parsley, then brush over the tops of the rolls.\xa0Cover with foil and bake for 10 minutes. Uncover and bake another 5 minutes until golden and heated through.
', '
Serve hot with a side of Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce for dipping.
\xa0
Find more ideas for Thanksgiving leftovers recipes and other holiday favorites on our recipes page!
']

Name: spicy-rosemary-sage-focaccia-recipe
Ingredients: ['2 cups all-purpose flour', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. sugar', '1 tsp. instant yeast', '¾ cup warm water', '2 Tbsp. olive oil (plus more for pan)', '1 Tbsp. chopped fresh rosemary', '1 Tbsp. chopped fresh sage']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil (divided)', 'Flaky salt and shredded parmesan, for garnish']
Procedure:
['
In a large bowl, mix flour, salt, sugar, and instant yeast. Add warm water and 2 tablespoons olive oil. Stir until a sticky dough forms, then knead in the bowl or on a floured surface for 3–5 minutes until smooth.
', '
Transfer dough to an oiled bowl, cover with a towel, and let rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.
', '
Oil a 9x13 baking dish or quarter sheet pan. Transfer dough into the pan and gently stretch it to the edges. Let rest for 20 minutes.
', '
Dimple the surface of the dough with your fingers, then sprinkle chopped rosemary and sage over the top. Spoon 1 tablespoon of Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil across the dough, letting it settle into the dimples.
', '
Bake at 425°F for 22–25 minutes until golden brown. Remove from oven and immediately drizzle the remaining 1 tablespoon of Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp over the top. Sprinkle with flaky salt and shredded parmesan and enjoy!
\xa0
Find more easy spicy recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: khao-soi-recipe---instant-ramen-hack
Ingredients: ['2 packages instant ramen noodles, with seasoning packets', '2 Tbsp. neutral oil', '1 shallot, chopped', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '2 cups chicken broth', '1 cup coconut milk', '2 chicken thighs, thinly sliced', '½ red onion, thinly sliced', 'Pickled mustard greens, chopped', 'Fresh cilantro', 'Lime wedges', 'Vegetable oil, for frying']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. red curry paste', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce', '½ tsp. ground turmeric', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '½ tsp. sugar', '1 tsp. fish sauce', 'Juice of ½ lime', 'Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, for drizzling']
Procedure:
['
Cook one pack of instant noodles, with seasoning packages removed, as directed. Drain, spread flat, and air dry or let sit until no longer wet. Fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Set aside for garnish.
', '
Heat coconut milk in a pot over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally. Cook until the mixture has split and oil is visible. Sauté shallot, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, and Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger until soft. Add red curry paste, Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce, and turmeric. Stir until fragrant.
', '
Pour in chicken broth and instant ramen seasoning packet and flakes and stir in Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce and sugar. Add sliced chicken and simmer until cooked through, about 10 minutes. Add fish sauce and lime juice to the broth.
', '
Cook a second pack of instant ramen in a separate pot of boiling water, seasoning packets removed. Divide noodles between bowls and ladle broth with chicken over top.
', "
Top each bowl with fried noodles, red onion, pickled mustard greens, fresh cilantro, lime wedges, and a generous drizzle of Lee Kum Kee\xa0Chiu Chow Style\xa0Chili Crisp Oil.\xa0
\xa0
We've got a whole bunch of other quick 30-minute Asian recipes on our recipes page, go check them out!
"]

Name: mille-feuille-nabe-hot-pot-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 head napa cabbage', '1 lb. thinly sliced pork belly', '12–16 fresh shiso leaves', '4 cups low-sodium dashi or chicken broth', '1/2 cup enoki mushrooms', '1/2 cup shimeji mushrooms', '2–3 shiitake mushrooms, scored on top', 'Cooked white rice, for serving']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '2 tsp. mirin', '2 tsp. sake (optional)', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', 'Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, for drizzling']
Marinade: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Procedure:
['
Mix Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, then rub onto each slice of pork belly. Let marinate while prepping veggies.
', '
Slice napa cabbage into halves lengthwise, then separate the leaves. Layer a cabbage leaf with a pork belly slice on top, and shiso leaves, alternating until you’ve built a full stack. Slice crosswise into 3-inch pieces.
', '
Tightly arrange the sliced stacks upright in a wide pot, forming a flower-like spiral pattern. Nestle the mushrooms in the center. 
', '
In a separate bowl, mix broth, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, mirin, sake, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, and Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger. Pour over the arranged ingredients until mostly submerged. Cover and simmer on medium heat for 20 minutes, or until everything is tender and juicy.
', '
Drizzle with Chili Crisp Oil before serving. Scoop into bowls and enjoy with hot white rice.
\xa0
Looking for more ideas for hot pot season? Check out our hot pot recipes page for ideas for other hot pot dishes and dipping sauces!
']

Name: spicy-potato-noodles-recipe
Ingredients: ['1.1 lb. russet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1‑inch pieces', '½ tsp. salt', '1½ cups potato starch', '½ cup warm water', '1 Tbsp. cilantro, minced']
Seasoning: ['Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, for drizzling']
Sauce Mix: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Mature Vinegar', '2 Tbsp. gochugaru (Korean red pepper flakes)', '1¼ tsp. granulated sugar', '⅛ tsp. salt', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 stalk green onion, sliced', '3 Tbsp. neutral oil']
Procedure:
['
Boil the potato chunks until fork‑tender, about 10‑15 minutes. Drain and mash them in a bowl with salt. While still hot, mix in potato starch and then warm water; knead until a cohesive, pliable dough forms.
', '
Divide the dough into 14 pieces, keeping covered with a damp towel. Roll each into a thick rope, about ½‑inch wide, shaping into noodles.
', '
Drop the noodles one at a time into a wide pot of boiling water. Once they float, cook for an additional minute. Remove and place into cool water while you cook the rest.
', '
In a bowl, combine Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Mature Vinegar, gochugaru, sugar, salt, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, and green onion. Heat neutral oil until shimmering, then carefully pour it over the sauce mix. Stir.
', '
Drain the noodles well and place them into a clean bowl. Pour the prepared sauce over them, toss until evenly coated. Garnish with chopped cilantro and a generous drizzle of Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil. Serve warm.
\xa0
For more easy Asian meal ideas, check out our recipes page!\xa0
']

Name: easy-jianbing-recipe-hack
Ingredients: ['Store-bought Chinese-style scallion pancakes ', '2 eggs ', '1 green onion, thinly sliced ', '1 tsp. black sesame seeds ', '2 crispy wonton chips ', '1 cup shredded chicken ', '1 small cucumber, julienned', 'Fresh cilantro leaves ']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce, for brushing ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, plus more for garnish ']
Procedure:
['
Heat the scallion pancake in a dry skillet over medium heat, turning once, until golden brown and crisp. Crack an egg into the center of the pancake and use a spatula to break the yolk and spread evenly to cover the surface.\xa0Add sliced green onions and black sesame seeds. Flip the pancake to the other side and reduce heat to low.
', '
Add Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce and Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil and use the back of a spoon to spread generously throughout the pancake.
', '
Place the crispy wonton chips in the center of the pancake, then add the shredded chicken, cucumber slices and cilantro leaves.\xa0Fold the left and right edges of the pancake over the filling, then fold the bottom edge up over the filling and roll toward the top to form a tight wrap. Slice in half to reveal the layers and drizzle with additional Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil. Serve while hot and crisp.
\xa0
Get more easy Asian recipe hacks on our recipes page!
']

Name: bang-bang-shrimp-recipe-with-mac-and-cheese
Ingredients: ['8 oz. elbow macaroni', 'Water, for boiling', '2 Tbsp. butter', '2 Tbsp. flour', '1 ¾ cups whole milk', '1 ½ cups shredded sharp cheddar cheese', '½ cup mozzarella cheese', '½ tsp. salt', '½ tsp. garlic powder', '¼ tsp. smoked paprika', '½ cup all-purpose flour', '½ cup cornstarch', '½ tsp. salt', '½ tsp. garlic powder', '½ lb. raw shrimp, peeled and deveined', '1 egg', 'Oil, for frying', 'Chopped chives, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, for drizzling']
Sauce Mix: ['¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo', '1 Tbsp. honey', '1 tsp. rice vinegar', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce']
Procedure:
['
Boil macaroni until al dente, drain, and set aside. In a saucepan, melt butter, whisk in flour, then slowly whisk in milk. Simmer until thickened, stir in cheeses, salt, garlic powder, and paprika. Mix in pasta and pour into a greased baking dish.
', '
Bake mac and cheese at 375°F for 20–25 minutes until golden and bubbly.
', '
While the mac and cheese bakes, whisk flour, cornstarch, salt, and garlic powder in a bowl. Dip shrimp in beaten egg, dredge in flour mixture, and deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
', '
Mix Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo, honey, vinegar, and Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce\xa0to make your bang bang sauce. Toss crispy shrimp in the bang bang sauce until evenly coated.
', '
Top baked mac with crispy shrimp, drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, and sprinkle with chopped chives.
\xa0
Add a little spice to your meals rotation with our list of spicy recipes!
']

Name: char-kway-teow-recipe---malaysian-style-noodles
Ingredients: ['2 Tbsp. neutral oil', '1 tsp Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '½ small onion, sliced', '4 oz. shrimp, peeled and deveined', '4 oz. Chinese fish cake, sliced', '4 oz. Chinese sausage (lap cheong), thinly sliced ', '2 oz. chicken thigh, thinly sliced', '1 egg', '8 oz. fresh flat rice noodles (hor fun)', '1½ cups bean sprouts', '1 cup Chinese chives, cut into 2-inch pieces', '2 Tbsp. water']
Sauce Mix: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce', '1 tsp. sugar']
Procedure:
['
In a small bowl, mix Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce,\xa0Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce, and sugar. Set aside.
', '
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large wok over high heat. Add Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and onion and stir-fry until fragrant. Toss in shrimp, chicken, sausage and fish cake, cooking until just done.
', '
Push everything to one side, add a little more oil, crack in the egg, scramble slightly, then stir everything together.
', '
Add rice noodles and sauce mix. Stir-fry quickly until noodles are evenly coated and slightly charred.
', '
Toss in bean sprouts, Chinese chives, and a splash of water. Stir-fry for 30 seconds more and serve immediately.
\xa0
Looking for other Asian noodle recipes? Visit our recipes page for ideas!\xa0
']

Name: gluten-free-hoisin-meatballs---holiday-party-recipe-by-chef-charles-chen
Ingredients: ['-- FOR THE MEATBALL MIX --', '1 ½ lbs. lean ground turkey (93% lean)', '½ cup gluten free panko breadcrumbs', '⅓ cup chopped scallions', '1 large egg', '-- FOR GARNISH --', '2 Tbsp. gluten free Panko breadcrumbs', '2 tsp. toasted sesame seeds for serving ', 'Chopped cilantro and scallions for serving ']
Seasoning: ['2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '4 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Gluten Free Hoisin Sauce', '1 tsp. pink Himalayan salt', '½ tsp. Lee Kum Kee Black Peppercorn Grinder']
Sauce Mix: ['¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Gluten Free Hoisin Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Green Label Oyster Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce', '1 Tbsp. lime juice', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Gluten Free Soy Sauce', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger']
Procedure:
['
Heat oven to 425°F (218ºC) and coat a baking sheet with nonstick spray. 
', '
Mix together the meatball ingredients and the seasoning ingredients in a large bowl. Grab about 1 1/2 oz. (about two heaping tablespoons) of the mixture, shape into a meatball, and add to the baking tray. Repeat for the rest of the meatball mix; this should make about 20 meatballs in total.\xa0
', '
Bake meatballs until browned and the internal temperature reaches 165ºF (74ºC), about 15–20 minutes. 
', '
While the meatballs bake, whisk together all of the sauce mix ingredients in a separate large bowl. Once the meatballs are done and cooled slightly, add to the bowl and toss gently to coat the meatballs.
', '
Combine fried shallots, panko and sesame seeds. Sprinkle\xa0the\xa0mixture, cilantro and scallions over meatballs before serving.
\xa0
Check out other holiday party recipes on our recipes page!\xa0
']

Name: butternut-squash-ravioli-recipe-with-hoisin-brown-butter-sage-sauce
Ingredients: ['-- FOR THE PASTA DOUGH --', '1 cup all-purpose flour', '1 large egg', '½ Tbsp. olive oil', 'Pinch of salt', '-- FOR THE FILLING --', '1½ cups cubed butternut squash', '¾ cup ricotta cheese', '¼ cup grated parmesan cheese', '1 egg yolk', 'Pinch of salt and black pepper', '-- FOR GARNISH --', 'Grated parmesan cheese', 'Chopped fresh sage', 'Red pepper flakes']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce (for brushing squash)']
Sauce Mix: ['½ cup unsalted butter', 'Fresh sage leaves', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', 'Pinch of salt and pepper']
Procedure:
['
On a clean surface, mound flour and make a well in the center. Add the egg, olive oil, and a pinch of salt. Gradually incorporate the flour with a fork until a dough forms. Knead 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Wrap and rest 30 minutes.
', '
Brush butternut squash cubes with Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce. Roast at 400°F for 25 minutes until tender and caramelized. Cool slightly, then transfer to a food processor with ricotta, parmesan, egg yolk, salt, and pepper. Blend until smooth.
', '
Roll out the rested dough into thin sheets. Cut into ravioli squares or rounds. Place 1 tsp filling in the center of each piece, top with another sheet of pasta, and press edges to seal.
', '
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add sage leaves and cook until the butter turns golden and nutty and the sage crisps. Lower heat and stir in Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce, whisking until incorporated. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
', '
Boil ravioli in salted water 2–3 minutes until they float. Transfer to the skillet and toss gently in the brown butter hoisin sage sauce. Plate and garnish with parmesan, fresh sage, and red pepper flakes.
\xa0
Looking for more ideas on how to cook with hoisin sauce? Check out our recipes page!
']

Name: chocolate-donut-recipe-with-chili-crisp-original-topping
Ingredients: ['2 (¼ oz) packages yeast', '¼ cup warm water (105–115°F)', '1½ cups lukewarm milk (scalded, then cooled)', '½ cup sugar', '1 tsp. salt', '2 large eggs', '½ cup shortening', '5 cups all-purpose flour', 'Canola oil, for frying']
Seasoning: ['Lee Kum Kee Original Chili Crisp, for drizzling', 'Flaky sea salt, for garnish (optional)']
Sauce Mix: ['-- For the Glaze --', '⅓ cup butter', '2 cups powdered sugar', '1½ tsp. vanilla extract', '4–6 Tbsp. hot water', '-- For the Chocolate Ganache --', '8 oz. semi-sweet chocolate, finely chopped', '1 cup heavy cream', '2 Tbsp. unsalted butter']
Procedure:
['
Dissolve yeast in warm water in a large bowl. Add milk, sugar, salt, eggs, shortening, and 2 cups flour; beat on low speed for 30 seconds, scraping bowl, then on medium speed for 2 minutes. Stir in remaining flour until smooth. Cover and let rise until doubled, 50–60 minutes.
', '
Turn dough onto a floured surface, lightly roll to coat, then roll out to ½-inch thickness. Cut with a floured doughnut cutter. Place on a floured surface, cover, and let rise until doubled, 30–40 minutes.
', '
Heat canola oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to 350°F. Fry doughnuts in batches until golden brown, about 1 minute per side. Remove carefully and drain on paper towels
', '
For the creamy glaze, melt butter in a small saucepan. Remove from heat and stir in powdered sugar and vanilla, adding hot water 1 Tbsp at a time until smooth. Dip warm doughnuts into glaze, place on a rack, and let set slightly.
', '
For the ganache, heat cream until just simmering. Pour over chopped chocolate in a heatproof bowl, let sit 2 minutes, then stir until smooth. Add butter and stir until glossy. 
\xa0
Spread ganache over glazed doughnuts and let cool before finishing.
', '
Sprinkle donuts with flaky sea salt if desired, then drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Original before serving.
\xa0
For more chili crisp recipe ideas to use Chili Crisp Original in, check out our product page!
']

Name: spicy-buttermilk-fried-chicken-recipe-with-chili-crisp-original
Ingredients: ['8 pieces chicken (drumsticks, thighs, breasts, or wings)', '2 cups all-purpose flour', '1 cup cornstarch', 'Peanut oil or neutral frying oil, for frying', 'Flaked salt, optional, for serving']
Seasoning: ['2 tsp. kosher salt', '2 tsp. paprika', '2 tsp. black pepper', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Original, for serving']
Marinade: ['2 tsp. kosher salt', '2 tsp. garlic powder', '2 tsp. onion powder', '2 cups buttermilk']
Procedure:
['
Place chicken pieces in a large bowl. Season with kosher salt, garlic powder, and onion powder; toss to coat. Add buttermilk. Stir to combine, cover, and refrigerate at least 2 hours or overnight.
', '
In a shallow dish, whisk together flour, cornstarch, 2 tsp kosher salt, paprika, and black pepper. Set aside.
', '
Fill a deep fryer or large heavy pot with oil to about 2–3 inches deep and heat to 325°F.
', '
Remove chicken from marinade, letting excess drip off. Dredge in the flour mixture until fully coated, shake off excess, then dip back into the buttermilk mixture (light coat) and dredge again in the flour mixture for a second coating. Let coated chicken rest for 3–5 minutes until the coating looks slightly pasty.
', '
Fry chicken in batches, turning occasionally, until golden brown and cooked through (165°F internal temperature), about 12–14 minutes for dark meat and 8–10 minutes for white meat. Drain on a paper towel–lined tray, sprinkle with flaked salt if desired, and drizzle or spoon Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Original\xa0over the top before serving.
\xa0
For more ways to use chili crisp, check out our product page!
']

Name: air-fryer-xo-fish-balls-in-honey
Ingredients: ['10 oz. fish paste']
Seasoning: ['3 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce (oil removed)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Procedure:
['
Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.
', '
Take a small portion (around 0.8 oz.) and knead as a ball. Repeat the step until all the mixture are done.
', '
Put the fish balls in the air-fryer. Air-fry them at 392°F for 10 minutes.
', '
Add in honey and toss well. Put it back to air-fryer again. Air-fry for 3 minutes at 356°F.
', '
Tips:
1. When cooking at home, wet your hands with water before you knead. It helps to avoid sticking your hands from the fish paste.

2. Prepare a plate with plastic wrap and brush a layer of oil. Then put the fish balls\xa0in step 2 on the plate. It helps to avoid sticking the plate from the fish balls.
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. baking soda    ', '1 tsp. cornstarch    ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce      ', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['2 oz. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. Chinese rice wine (or white wine)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon ', '1/3 cup water', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
PREPARE THE BROCCOLI. Blanch the broccoli in hot salted water for about 2 minutes or until slightly tender and dark green. Shock it in ice water and drain thoroughly.\xa0\xa0
', '
MARINATE THE BEEF. In a small bowl, whisk together the baking soda, cornstarch, Lee Kum Kee Cooking Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Pour the mixed sauces over your thinly sliced beef flank steak and let it sit for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
\xa0
', '
CREATE THE SAUCE MIX. In a separate small bowl, stir together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Chinese rice wine, Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon, water, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
', '
STIR-FRY. Heat a wok or a large skillet on high heat and add the oil. When a wisp of white smoke appears, toss the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and marinated beef into the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, continually moving the beef slices so they are brown on all sides, but still rare. Once the beef is seared, add the broccoli and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the sauce and keep it all moving.
', '
FINISH AND SERVE. Don’t be afraid to scrape any bits off the bottom of the pan before it starts to burn. Once the sauce has turned into a nice thick glaze, serve immediately with garnishes (optional). Enjoy!
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
