Name: korean-spicy-pork-bibimbap-recipe-with-chili-crisp-smoky
Ingredients: ['1 lb. pork shoulder or pork belly, thinly sliced', '-- For the Vegetables: --', '2 cups spinach', '1 cup bean sprouts', '1 cup zucchini, julienned', '1 cup carrots, julienned', '1 tsp.  Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil (per vegetable)', 'Pinch of salt (per vegetable)', '-- For the Bowl: --', '4 cups cooked short-grain white rice (keep warm)', '4 eggs', '2 tsp Oil (for frying eggs)', '1 Tbsp. sesame seeds']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Smoky', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Gochujang', '1 Tbsp. brown sugar', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, mix Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Smoky, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, gochujang, brown sugar, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil to make the marinade. Add pork slices and toss to coat thoroughly. Marinate for 20 minutes while preparing vegetables.
', '
Blanch spinach and bean sprouts separately in boiling water for 30 seconds each, then shock in ice water and squeeze dry. Sauté zucchini and carrots individually over medium heat with 1 tsp Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil and a pinch of salt for 1–2 minutes each until tender-crisp. Set each component aside in neat piles.
', '
Heat a heavy skillet or wok over medium-high heat until hot. Cook the marinated pork in batches, letting it sear undisturbed for 1–2 minutes per side until caramelized and slightly charred. Transfer to a plate and keep warm.
', '
In the same skillet, reduce heat to medium and fry eggs sunny-side up in oil until the edges are crisp and golden, leaving yolks runny for mixing later.
', '
Divide hot rice among 4 bowls and arrange spinach, bean sprouts, zucchini, carrots, and spicy pork on top in sections. Place a fried egg in the center of each bowl, drizzle generously with Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Smoky, and sprinkle with sesame seeds. Serve immediately, mixing thoroughly before eating.
\xa0
For more Asian fusion recipes, head to our recipes page!
']

Name: bbq-ribs-recipe-with-smoky-chili-crisp-glaze
Ingredients: ['2 racks baby back pork ribs (about 4 lbs. total)', '1 Tbsp. toasted sesame seeds', '2 green onions, thinly sliced']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. black pepper', '2 tsp. smoked paprika', '1 tsp. garlic powder', '½ tsp. ground cumin']
Sauce Mix: ['½ cup Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Smoky, plus extra for serving', '2 Tbsp. honey', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar']
Procedure:
['
Preheat oven to 300°F. Remove the thin silver skin membrane from the back of the ribs and pat ribs dry with paper towels.
', '
In a small bowl, mix kosher salt, black pepper, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and ground cumin. Rub this spice mix evenly over both sides of the ribs.
', '
Place the ribs meat-side up on a large foil-lined baking sheet. Cover tightly with aluminum foil to seal in moisture. Bake for 2 hours until the ribs are tender but not falling apart.
', '
While the ribs bake, make the glaze by combining Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Smoky, honey, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar in a small saucepan over medium heat. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
', '
After 2 hours, remove the ribs from the oven and discard the foil. Increase oven temperature to 425°F. Brush the glaze generously over the ribs. Return to the oven uncovered and bake for an additional 10–15 minutes, basting once halfway through, until the glaze is sticky and caramelized. Transfer ribs to a cutting board and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and sliced green onions. Drizzle with extra Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Smoky\xa0and enjoy!
\xa0
Chili Crisp Smoky goes great on any BBQ recipe - get some more ideas on our recipes page!
']

Name: nam-khao-recipe-for-lao-style-crispy-rice-salad
Ingredients: ['-- FOR THE RICE BALLS --', '2 cups jasmine rice', 'Salt, to taste', '2½ cups water', '1 large egg', '3 Tbsp. red curry paste', '1 cup dried unsweetened shredded coconut', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '2 Tbsp. shallots, minced', '1 Tbsp. brown sugar', '2 Tbsp. Makrut lime leaves, very finely chopped', '-- FOR MIXING WITH THE RICE AFTER FRYING --', '1 cup cured pork sausage (Naem), diced', '½ cup fresh cilantro, chopped', '½ cup green onions, finely sliced', '½ cup mint, finely chopped', '¼ cup roasted peanuts, lightly crushed', 'Lettuce leaves, for serving', 'Lime wedges, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Cilantro, plus extra for drizzling', '2 Tbsp. fish sauce', 'Juice of 1 whole lime']
Procedure:
['
Cook and cool the rice:\xa0Rinse jasmine rice thoroughly until the water runs clear. Combine rice and water in a medium pot, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes until the rice is tender and fluffy. Spread cooked rice onto a tray and let cool completely for 1 hour.
\xa0
*TIP: Alternatively, you can cook the Jasmine rice in a rice cooker. Make sure to remove and add to the tray to cool as soon as it is finished.\xa0
', '
Mix and prepare the rice ball mixture: In a small bowl, whisk together the egg, red curry paste, and brown sugar until smooth. Transfer cooled rice into a large mixing bowl and stir in the curry-egg mixture, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, shredded unsweetened dried coconut, finely chopped Makrut lime leaves, and minced shallots until fully combined.
', '
Deep fry the rice balls:\xa0With slightly damp hands, form the rice mixture into golf ball-sized balls. Heat vegetable oil in a deep pan to 350°F and fry the rice balls for 8–10 minutes until golden and crispy. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate and let cool slightly.
', '
Break up the rice balls and season:\xa0Place the crispy rice balls in a large bowl and break them apart into bite-sized crumbles. Add fish sauce, lime juice, and Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Cilantro, tossing gently to coat. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
', '
Fold in remaining ingredients and serve:\xa0Fold in diced cured pork sausage, chopped cilantro and mint, green onions, and roasted peanuts. Serve with lettuce leaves and garnish with lime wedges and an extra drizzle of Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Cilantro.
To enjoy, add scoops of the Nam Khao to the lettuce leaves, squeeze over lime juice, add more chili crisp if desired, and enjoy like a lettuce wrap!\xa0
\xa0
For more ways to try Lee Kum Kee\xa0Chili Crisp Cilantro, head to the product page!
']

Name: chipotle-chicken-alfredo-pasta-recipe-with-chili-crisp-cilantro
Ingredients: ['12 oz. fettuccine pasta', '1½ lbs. boneless, skinless chicken breasts', '1 Tbsp olive oil', '2 Tbsp. chopped fresh cilantro', '1 lime, cut into wedges']
Seasoning: ['1 tsp. smoked paprika', '½ tsp. salt', '¼ tsp. black pepper', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Cilantro, for garnish']
Sauce Mix: ['3 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 chipotle pepper (from a can of chipotles in adobo sauce), finely chopped', '2 Tbsp. all-purpose flour', '2 cups heavy cream', '½ cup grated Parmesan cheese', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 Tbsp. adobo sauce, from a 7 oz. can of chipotles in adobo sauce']
Procedure:
['
Cook fettuccine in a large pot of salted boiling water according to package directions until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta water and drain the rest.
', '
Season chicken breasts on both sides with smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken for 5–6 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through. Transfer to a cutting board and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing into strips.
', '
In the same skillet, melt butter over medium heat. Add Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and chopped chipotle pepper and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant. Whisk in flour and cook for 1 minute to form a roux. Slowly pour in heavy cream, whisking constantly until smooth. Simmer for 2–3 minutes until slightly thickened. Stir in Parmesan cheese, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce, and adobo sauce until the sauce is creamy and glossy.
', '
Add the cooked fettuccine to the sauce, tossing gently to coat. If needed, add reserved pasta water a little at a time to loosen the sauce.
', '
Divide the pasta among bowls and top with sliced chicken. Garnish with Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Cilantro, chopped fresh cilantro, and a squeeze of lime. Serve immediately.
\xa0
Visit our recipes page for more spicy and creamy pasta recipes!
']

Name: spicy-salmon-crispy-rice-bites-recipe-with-chili-crisp-original
Ingredients: ['2 cups cooked sushi rice, cooled', 'Vegetable oil, for frying', '12 bite-size cubes (about 1-inch) of salmon fillet', '½ avocado, thinly sliced', '1 small cucumber, thinly sliced', '1 jalapeño, thinly sliced', '1 tsp. sesame seeds']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar', '½ tsp. sugar', '½ tsp. salt']
Marinade: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. lime juice', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '½ tsp. salt', '½ tsp. black pepper']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, mix cooled sushi rice with Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar, sugar, and salt. Line a square pan with plastic wrap and press rice evenly into the pan to about ½-inch thickness. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes to firm up.
', '
Cut chilled rice into 12 small rectangles. Heat ½-inch vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Fry rice rectangles in batches for 2–3 minutes per side until golden and crispy. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain.
', '
In a bowl, toss salmon cubes with Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, lime juice, Lee\xa0Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, salt, and black pepper. Let marinate for 10 minutes. Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat and sear salmon cubes for 1–2 minutes per side until cooked through and caramelized.
', '
In a small bowl, combine Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Original and Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo Dressing/Spread. Gently toss cooked salmon in sauce then place a salmon cube on each crispy rice rectangle. Top with avocado, cucumber, and jalapeño slices.
', '
Drizzle more Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Original\xa0over the assembled bites and sprinkle with sesame seeds. Serve immediately while warm.
\xa0
Get more salmon recipes on our seafood recipes page!
']

Name: burrata-with-prosciutto-flatbread-recipe-with-chili-crisp-original
Ingredients: ['2 tavern-style flatbreads ', '1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese', '4 slices prosciutto', '1 cup baby arugula', '1 large burrata ball (about 4 oz)']
Seasoning: ['3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Original']
Procedure:
['
Preheat oven to 400°F. Place flatbreads on a baking sheet. Spread 1 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Original\xa0evenly over each flatbread as the base layer.
', '
Sprinkle mozzarella evenly over both flatbreads. Bake for 8–10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the edges of the flatbread are golden.
', '
Remove from the oven and immediately top each flatbread with 2 slices of prosciutto and a handful of arugula.
', '
Gently place half the burrata ball in the center of each flatbread. Drizzle with 1 Tbsp.\xa0Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Original and balsamic glaze.
', '
Slice and serve warm, letting the creamy burrata ooze over the flatbread as you cut.
\xa0
For more recipes and information on Chili Crisp Original, check out its product page!
']

Name: moo-krata-by-miso-hungrry-thai-bbq-and-hot-pot-recipe
Ingredients: ['--PROTEINS--', '1/2 lb. Pork belly, thinly sliced', '1 lb. Pork shoulder or loin, thinly sliced (for marinating)', '1 lb. Brisket/sirloin, thinly sliced ', '1 lb. Shrimp, head-on', '1/2 lb. Squid or cuttlefish, scored and cut into rings', '1/2 lb. Fish balls, meatballs, or shrimp balls', '--VEGETABLES--', '2 cups Chinese cabbage (napa cabbage) leaves, separated', '2 cups Morning glory (pak boong, water spinach)', '2 cups Baby corn', '2 cups Carrot slices', '2 cups Pumpkin or squash slices', '2 cups Mushrooms (enoki, oyster, or straw mushrooms)', '2 cups Lettuce leaves', '--NOODLES AND STARCH--', '4 oz. Glass noodles (mung bean vermicelli, soaked)', 'Instant noodles as desired', 'Cooked White Rice, for serving (1 cup per person)']
Marinade: ['1 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce ', '1 tsp. sugar (palm sugar if possible, or white sugar)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic ', '¼ tsp. Lee Kum Kee White Pepper Powder', '½ tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
Make the marinade for the thinly sliced pork loin (Use the ingredients from the "Marinade" section):

Pound Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic into a paste
Add Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce and Premium Soy Sauce into a bowl, then add the sugar, garlic, and white pepper.
Mix together thoroughly, then while whisking, add cornstarch so it dissolves thoroughly.
Coat the thinly sliced pork loin in the marinade and marinate for at least 30 minutes. 2-3 hours or overnight is optimal.

', '
Make the Moo Krata / Suki Dipping Sauce (use ingredients from the "Dipping" section):

Pound cilantro root, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, and Thai bird's eye chili (prik kee noo) into a paste.
In a bowl, combine Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha, Premium Oyster Sauce, Seasoned Rice Vinegar, Sodium Reduced Seasoned Soy Sauce, ketchup, and sugar, until the sugar dissolves.
Stir in the garlic-cilantro-chili paste, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, and water. Mix well, then finish with sesame seeds.
Transfer to dipping sauce bowls as needed per person.

', '
Prepare the Moo Krata Spread for enjoying:
\xa0

Add water or broth to the hot pot portion of your appliance, and turn both heating elements on.
Once hot, start grilling the marinated pork loin while adding vegetables as desired to the hot pot to flavor the broth.
Cook meats, other veggies and noodles as desired.\xa0
Once something is cooked on the grill or in the hot pot, dip in the dipping sauce and enjoy!

For more inspirations for Thai food, hot pot, or BBQ, make sure to follow our recipes page!
']

Name: halloween-inari-sushi-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 cup uncooked sushi rice', '1¼ cups water', '2 Tbsp. rice vinegar', '1 Tbsp. sugar', '10 store-bought inari age (fried tofu pockets)', '½ cup finely grated carrot', '½ small avocado, mashed with a squeeze of lime', '1 Tbsp. sesame seeds (optional)', '1 sheet nori (for decorating faces)', '10 edamame beans (for “pumpkin stems”)']
Seasoning: ['1½ tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '¾ cup dashi stock', '2 Tbsp. mirin', '2 Tbsp. sugar']
Procedure:
['
Rinse the rice and cook it in a rice cooker or pot with 1¼ cups water until tender. Let cool slightly, then season with rice vinegar and 1 Tbsp sugar. Stir gently to combine.
', '
In a saucepan, combine Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, dashi stock, mirin, and 2 Tbsp sugar. Bring to a simmer, then add the inari age. Simmer gently for 12–15 minutes until the pockets absorb the flavors. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
', '
Divide the seasoned rice into three bowls:

Pumpkin rice: leave plain white.
Carrot pumpkin rice: stir in finely grated carrot until rice turns orange.
Avocado monster rice: mash avocado with a squeeze of lime and fold into the rice until green.

', '
Gently open each inari pouch and fill with 2–3 Tbsp of your chosen rice, shaping into plump pumpkins or monsters.
', '
Cut jack-o’-lantern faces, monster eyes, or spooky mouths from the nori sheet. Press onto the surface of the inari to decorate. Top pumpkins with edamame “stems” before serving. Serve chilled or at room temperature with Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo for dipping.
\xa0
Get holiday recipes for Halloween or any other special occasion on our recipes page!\xa0
']

Name: mini-pumpkin-breakfast-boats-recipe
Ingredients: ['4 mini pumpkins (about 1–1½ pounds each)', '¼ cup cooked breakfast sausage, chopped', '¼ cup crumbled bacon', '½ cup shredded mozzarella', '½ cup shredded cheddar', '4 large eggs', 'Salt and pepper to taste', 'Microgreens, sage leaves, or croutons for garnish']
Seasoning: ['4 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, for drizzling']
Procedure:
['
Preheat oven to 375°F. Slice the tops off each pumpkin and scoop out the seeds and stringy bits. Lightly season the insides with salt and pepper.\xa0Brush 1 teaspoon Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce inside each pumpkin.
', '
Divide the cooked sausage and bacon evenly among the pumpkins, then sprinkle in shredded cheeses. Crack one egg into each pumpkin, being careful not to break the yolk. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
', '
Place pumpkins on a baking sheet and bake uncovered for 35–45 minutes, or until egg whites are set and yolks are cooked to your liking. Tent with foil if the tops brown too quickly.
', "
Drizzle each baked pumpkin with Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, then garnish with microgreens, sage leaves, and croutons. Serve warm.
\xa0
We've got plenty more breakfast recipes packed with Asian flavors, don't forget to check them out!
"]

Name: steak-bites-recipe-with-spicy-garlic-parm-sauce
Ingredients: ['1½ lbs. sirloin or ribeye steak, cut into 1½-inch cubes', '1 Tbsp. olive oil']
Marinade: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce']
Sauce Mix: ['2 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '2 Tbsp. grated Parmesan cheese', '1 Tbsp. chopped parsley', '½ tsp. Lee Kum Kee Coarse Ground Black Pepper']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, toss steak cubes with Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce and Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce. You can use these straight away but for max flavor, marinate at room temperature for 15–30 minutes, or cover and refrigerate up to 4 hours.
', '
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear steak bites in batches (don’t overcrowd) for 1–2 minutes per side until golden and caramelized. Transfer to a plate and keep warm.
', '
In the same pan, reduce heat to medium and add butter, Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce and Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic. Stir for 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
', '
Return steak bites to the skillet and toss to coat in the spicy garlic butter. Add black pepper and Parmesan cheese and stir until melted and glossy.
', '
\xa0Sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve immediately with garlic noodles.
\xa0
For more Asian inspired steak recipes, check out our recipes page!
']

Name: taiwanese-style-popcorn-chicken-recipe-with-spicy-smoky-bbq-sauce
Ingredients: ['1½ lbs. boneless skin-on chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch cubes', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Smoky BBQ Flavored Sriracha Sauce', '1 large egg', '3 Tbsp. rice flour', '2 cups tapioca starch (or potato starch)', '1½ cups Thai basil leaves', 'Vegetable oil, for frying', 'Salt and ground white pepper, to taste']
Marinade: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Chinese cooking wine', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 tsp. five-spice powder']
Procedure:
['
In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, Chinese cooking wine, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, and five-spice powder. Toss to coat and marinate for 20 minutes.
', '
Mix egg, rice flour, and 2 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Smoky BBQ Flavored Sriracha Sauce\xa0into the marinated chicken to create a flavorful wet batter. Dredge each piece in tapioca starch, pressing gently to coat evenly, and let rest for 5–7 minutes to help the crust stick.
', '
Heat vegetable oil in a deep pan to 350°F. Fry Thai basil leaves for 30–60 seconds until crispy, then remove and drain on paper towels.
', '
Working in batches, fry chicken for 4–5 minutes until golden brown and cooked through. Increase heat slightly and fry chicken a second time for 1–2 minutes for an extra crispy texture.
', '
Immediately sprinkle the hot chicken with salt and ground white pepper to taste. Arrange chicken and crispy basil on a serving platter and serve with Lee Kum Kee Smoky BBQ Flavored Sriracha Sauce\xa0for dipping.
\xa0
Check out more Asian inspired chicken recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: bbq-chicken-bowl-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 cup jasmine rice, rinsed', '2 cups water', '1½ lbs. boneless skinless chicken thighs', '1 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '½ tsp. salt', '¼ tsp. black pepper', '1 cup shredded red cabbage', '1 cup shredded carrots', '1 avocado, sliced', '2 green onions, sliced', '1 Tbsp. toasted sesame seeds']
Sauce Mix: ['½ cup Lee Kum Kee Smoky BBQ Sriracha Chilli Sauce', '1 Tbsp. honey', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar']
Procedure:
['
In a medium saucepan, combine jasmine rice and water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15–18 minutes until the rice is tender and water is absorbed. Remove from heat and let sit covered for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
\xa0
Tip: If you have a rice cooker, you can add the rice and water to the rice cooker instead and cook on the "white rice" setting.
', '
While the rice cooks, season chicken thighs with salt and black pepper. Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and cook for 5–6 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 165°F).
', '
In a small saucepan, combine Lee Kum Kee Smoky BBQ Sriracha Chilli Sauce, honey, and Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar. Bring to a gentle simmer over low heat for 2–3 minutes, stirring frequently, until slightly thickened.
', '
Brush the warm sticky glaze over the chicken during the last 2 minutes of cooking in the skillet, allowing it to caramelize slightly. Transfer chicken to a cutting board and let rest for 3 minutes before slicing into strips.
', '
Divide cooked rice between 4 bowls. Top each with sliced chicken, shredded cabbage, shredded carrots, and avocado slices. Drizzle any extra sticky glaze over the bowls and sprinkle with green onions and toasted sesame seeds. Serve warm.
\xa0
Check out our recipes page for meal prep recipe ideas!
']

Name: air-fryer-xo-fish-balls-in-honey
Ingredients: ['10 oz. fish paste']
Seasoning: ['3 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce (oil removed)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Procedure:
['
Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.
', '
Take a small portion (around 0.8 oz.) and knead as a ball. Repeat the step until all the mixture are done.
', '
Put the fish balls in the air-fryer. Air-fry them at 392°F for 10 minutes.
', '
Add in honey and toss well. Put it back to air-fryer again. Air-fry for 3 minutes at 356°F.
', '
Tips:
1. When cooking at home, wet your hands with water before you knead. It helps to avoid sticking your hands from the fish paste.

2. Prepare a plate with plastic wrap and brush a layer of oil. Then put the fish balls\xa0in step 2 on the plate. It helps to avoid sticking the plate from the fish balls.
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. baking soda    ', '1 tsp. cornstarch    ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce      ', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['2 oz. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. Chinese rice wine (or white wine)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon ', '1/3 cup water', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
PREPARE THE BROCCOLI. Blanch the broccoli in hot salted water for about 2 minutes or until slightly tender and dark green. Shock it in ice water and drain thoroughly.\xa0\xa0
', '
MARINATE THE BEEF. In a small bowl, whisk together the baking soda, cornstarch, Lee Kum Kee Cooking Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Pour the mixed sauces over your thinly sliced beef flank steak and let it sit for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
\xa0
', '
CREATE THE SAUCE MIX. In a separate small bowl, stir together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Chinese rice wine, Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon, water, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
', '
STIR-FRY. Heat a wok or a large skillet on high heat and add the oil. When a wisp of white smoke appears, toss the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and marinated beef into the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, continually moving the beef slices so they are brown on all sides, but still rare. Once the beef is seared, add the broccoli and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the sauce and keep it all moving.
', '
FINISH AND SERVE. Don’t be afraid to scrape any bits off the bottom of the pan before it starts to burn. Once the sauce has turned into a nice thick glaze, serve immediately with garnishes (optional). Enjoy!
']

Name: halloween-eyeball-pizza-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 ball store-bought pizza dough (enough for one 12-inch pizza)', 'Flour, for dusting', '½ cup marinara sauce', '1½ cups shredded mozzarella cheese', '16–20 slices pepperoni', '8–10 mini mozzarella balls or sliced mozzarella rounds', '8–10 black olive slices (from whole pitted olives)']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce', 'Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce, for drizzling']
Procedure:
['
Preheat oven to 475°F. Lightly flour a surface and stretch the pizza dough into a 12-inch round. Place on a parchment-lined baking sheet or pizza stone.
', '
In a small bowl, stir together marinara sauce and Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce until fully combined. Spread the spicy marinara evenly over the dough, leaving a ½-inch crust.
', '
Sprinkle shredded mozzarella over the sauce, then evenly place pepperoni slices across the pizza.
', '
Cut mozzarella balls in half and place one half in the center of each pepperoni slice. 
', '
Bake for 12–15 minutes, or until the crust is golden and cheese is bubbly and slightly browned. Let cool for 2 minutes, then top each mozzarella ball with a sliced black olive “pupil” to create the eyeball effect. Finish with a drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce before slicing and serving.
\xa0
Check out our Holiday Recipes page for more Halloween recipe ideas!
']

Name: cilantro-lime-salmon-bites-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 lb. skinless salmon fillet, cut into 1½-inch cubes', '1 Tbsp. olive oil', '1 tsp. salt', '½ tsp. smoked paprika', '¼ tsp. black pepper', '1 Tbsp. chopped fresh cilantro, for garnish']
Sauce Mix: ['¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Cilantro Lime Sriracha Chilli Sauce', '2 Tbsp. mayonnaise', '1 Tbsp. lime juice']
Procedure:
['
Pat salmon cubes dry with paper towels. Season evenly with salt, smoked paprika, and black pepper.
', '
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add salmon cubes and sear for 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through. Transfer to a plate.
', '
In a small bowl, whisk together Lee Kum Kee Cilantro Lime Flavored Sriracha Sauce, mayonnaise and lime juice until smooth and creamy.
', '
Arrange salmon bites on a platter. Spoon a little of the creamy sauce over each piece. Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
\xa0
Find more salmon recipes for meal prep and more on our seafood recipes page!
']

Name: cilantro-lime-chicken-pho-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 Tbsp vegetable oil', '1 large onion, halved', '3-inch piece fresh ginger, sliced', '4 cups chicken broth', '4 cups water', '1½ lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs', '1 tsp sugar', '1 cinnamon stick', '4 whole star anise', '4 whole cloves', '1 Tbsp fish sauce', '8 oz dried rice noodles (pho noodles)', '--FOR SERVING--', '1 cup bean sprouts', '1 cup fresh Thai basil leaves', '1 cup fresh cilantro leaves', '1 lime, cut into wedges', '2 green onions, sliced', '1 jalapeño, thinly sliced']
Seasoning: ['¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Cilantro Lime Sriracha Chilli Sauce', '¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce']
Procedure:
['
Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Sear onion and ginger, cut side down, for 3–4 minutes until charred and fragrant.
', '
Add chicken broth, water, chicken thighs, sugar, cinnamon stick, star anise, and cloves. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 30–35 minutes. Skim off any foam as needed.
', '
Remove chicken from broth and shred into bite-sized pieces; discard skin and bones. Strain broth through a fine mesh sieve and return to pot and mix in fish sauce. Keep warm.\xa0
', '
Cook rice noodles according to package directions. Drain and rinse under cold water to prevent sticking.
', '
Divide noodles between bowls, top with shredded chicken, and ladle hot broth over. Garnish with bean sprouts, basil, cilantro, lime, green onions, jalapeño, and drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Cilantro Lime Flavored Sriracha Sauce and serve with Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce. 
\xa0
Enjoy, and check out more easy chicken recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: cheesy-breakfast-hotteok-recipe-with-honey-sriracha
Ingredients: ['¾ cup warm water', '2 teaspoons SAF instant yeast', '¼ cup warm milk', '2 Tbsp. sugar', '1 Tbsp. melted butter', '2 ½ Tbsp. neutral oil', '½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil ', '4 Tbsp. glutinous rice flour', '1 tsp. kosher salt', '2 cups bread flour', '1 tsp. baking powder', '½ cup cooked bacon bits', '¾ cup mozzarella + ¼ cup Monterey Jack, combined']
Procedure:
['
In a mixing bowl, combine warm water, SAF instant yeast, warm milk, and sugar. Stir well and let bloom for 5 minutes. Add melted butter, neutral oil,\xa0Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, glutinous rice flour, salt, bread flour, and baking powder. Mix and knead for about 5 minutes until the dough is smooth and gluten begins to develop.
', '
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the dough rise in a warm area for 45 to 75 minutes, until it has doubled in size. For easier shaping, refrigerate the dough for 1 hour to firm it up (optional but recommended).
', '
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the dough rise in a warm area for 45 to 75 minutes, until it has doubled in size. For easier shaping, refrigerate the dough for 1 hour to firm it up (optional but recommended).
', '
Heat a pan over medium to medium-high heat with a light coating of oil. Place each sealed dough ball seam-side down and press gently with a spatula until it’s about 1½ inches thick. Cook until golden brown on both sides and the cheese is melted inside, about 3–4 minutes per side.
', '
Drizzle with\xa0Lee Kum Kee\xa0Honey Sriracha or serve on the side for dipping.
\xa0
Check out other Asian inspired breakfast recipes here!
']

Name: sweet-and-spicy-wings-recipe-with-gochujang-honey-sriracha
Ingredients: ['2 lbs. chicken wings', '1 Tbsp. toasted sesame seeds', '2 green onions, thinly sliced']
Marinade: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. cornstarch']
Sauce Mix: ['¼ cup gochujang (Korean chili paste)', '¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger']
Procedure:
['
In a large bowl, toss chicken wings with Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, and cornstarch until evenly coated. Cover and marinate for at least 30 minutes or up to overnight.

', '
Preheat oven to 425°F. Place wings on a wire rack over a foil-lined baking sheet. Bake for 35–40 minutes, flipping halfway through, until golden and crispy.
', '
While wings bake, whisk together gochujang,\xa0 Lee Kum Kee\xa0Honey Sriracha Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, and Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger in a small saucepan. Heat over medium until bubbling, then remove from heat.

', '
In a small bowl, stir\xa0Lee Kum Kee Chiu\xa0Chow Chili Crisp Oil into ranch dressing and chill until ready to serve.

', '
Toss hot wings in the gochujang sriracha honey glaze until fully coated. Sprinkle with sesame seeds and green onions. Serve hot with chili crisp ranch on the side.
\xa0
For more gameday recipe ideas infused with Asian flavors, head to our appetizer recipes page!
']

Name: autumn-panzanella-salad-recipe
Ingredients: ['2 cups cubed butternut squash', '2 cups halved brussels sprouts', '2 cups rustic bread, cut into 1-inch cubes', '1 Tbsp. olive oil ', '1 cup baby spinach or arugula', '¼ cup pomegranate seeds', '2 Tbsp. roasted pepitas (pumpkin seeds)', 'Fresh chopped sage or rosemary for garnish']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. olive oil', '1/2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', 'Salt and pepper to taste']
Procedure:
['
Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss butternut squash and brussels sprouts with olive oil, ½ Tbsp Lee Kum Kee\xa0Hoisin Sauce, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 25–30 minutes, flipping halfway, until caramelized and tender.


', '
On a separate baking sheet, toss bread cubes with olive oil. Toast in the oven during the last 10–12 minutes of roasting until golden and crisp.
', '
In a small bowl, whisk together remaining Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce,\xa0Lee Kum Kee\xa0Premium Soy Sauce, balsamic vinegar, maple syrup, olive oil, and Dijon mustard until smooth.

', '
In a large bowl, combine roasted vegetables, toasted bread cubes, spinach, pomegranate seeds, and pepitas. Drizzle with the hoisin-soy dressing and toss gently to coat.
', '
Plate the salad and garnish with fresh chopped sage or rosemary. Serve warm or at room temperature.
\xa0
Check out more Asian inspired salad recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: black-cat-halloween-fried-fish-sandwich-recipe
Ingredients: ['2 4 oz. white fish filets (tilapia, cod, or haddock, for example)', '½ cup all-purpose flour', '1 egg, beaten', '1 cup panko breadcrumbs', 'Oil for frying', '2 charcoal (black) brioche buns', '2 slices American cheese', '½ cup shredded cabbage', '4 pickle slices', 'American cheese slices, cut into “cat feature” shapes (ears, whiskers, nose)', '2 thin daikon slices (for eyes)', 'Small pieces of nori (for pupils)', 'Curly fries, for serving']
Seasoning: ['¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo']
Marinade: ['½ tsp garlic powder', 'Salt and pepper to taste', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce']
Procedure:
['
Pat the fish fillets dry and season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Brush with Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce and marinate for 15 minutes.
', '
Dredge each fillet in flour, dip into the beaten egg, and coat thoroughly with panko breadcrumbs.
', '
Heat ½ inch of vegetable oil in a skillet over medium heat. Fry the fish for 3–4 minutes per side, or until golden and crispy. Transfer to a rack or paper towel-lined plate.
', '
Toast the charcoal buns. Spread the bottom buns with Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo, then layer with shredded cabbage, the fried fish, one slice of American cheese, pickles, more Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo, then cap with the bun top.
', '
Cut American cheese to form the cat ears, whiskers, and nose. Punch out small circles of daikon for eyes and press nori pieces into the centers for pupils. Press the daikon-nori eyes into the top buns along with the cheese features. Serve hot with curly fries.
\xa0
Get more inspiration for Halloween and other holidays with our holiday recipe ideas!
']

Name: creamy-coconut-curry-orzo-recipe-with-chicken-meatballs
Ingredients: ['1 lb. ground chicken', '2 green onions, finely chopped', '1 cup orzo', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 Tbsp. red curry paste', '1 (13.5 oz) can full-fat coconut milk', '1½ cups chicken broth', '1 cup Thai basil leaves (or cilantro), plus more for garnish', 'Juice of ½ lime']
Seasoning: ['Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, for drizzling']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '½ tsp. salt', '¼ tsp. black pepper']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, combine ground chicken, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, green onions, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger, salt, and pepper. Mix until just combined, then form into 12 small meatballs.
', '
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add the meatballs and cook, turning occasionally, until golden on all sides and cooked through, about 8–10 minutes. Remove and set aside.
', '
In the same skillet, pour in the coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook until it begins to split and release its fat, 3–5 minutes. Add 1 tsp Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and red curry paste. Stir and cook out for 2–3 minutes until fragrant.
', '
Stir in the orzo, then pour in the chicken broth. Bring to a simmer and cook, stirring often, for 8–10 minutes until orzo is tender and sauce is creamy. Stir in Thai basil (or cilantro) and lime juice.
', '
Nestle the cooked meatballs back into the orzo and let everything warm through for 2–3 minutes. Plate and drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Chili Crisp Oil. Garnish with more basil and serve.
\xa0
Check out our many other easy Asian inspired dinner recipes to add to your lineup!
']

Name: chicken-breakfast-biscuit-sandwich-recipe-with-chili-crisp-hot-honey
Ingredients: ['-- For the Fried Chicken --', '2 boneless, skinless chicken thighs', '1 cup all-purpose flour', '1/2 tsp garlic powder', '1/2 tsp paprika', 'Vegetable oil, for frying', '-- For Sandwich Assembly --', '4 biscuits, split and toasted', '4 eggs', '4 slices American or cheddar cheese', 'Pickles (optional)']
Seasoning: ['Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey Sauce, for drizzling']
Marinade: ['1 cup buttermilk', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce', '1 tsp. salt', '1 tsp. black pepper']
Sauce Mix: ['1/4 cup Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey Sauce', '1 Tbsp. butter']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, combine buttermilk,\xa0Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce, salt, and pepper. Butterfly or pound out chicken thighs, add to marinade, and let sit for at least 15 minutes.
', '
In a shallow dish, mix flour, garlic powder, and paprika. Dredge marinated chicken in flour mixture, pressing to coat well.
', '
Heat about 1 inch of oil in a skillet over medium heat to 350°F. Fry chicken thighs for 4–5 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. Drain on a wire rack.
', '
In a small saucepan, warm Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey Sauce with butter over low heat until melted and glossy. Brush or pour generously over hot fried chicken.
', '
Fry eggs in oil one at a time. Top with a slice of cheese to melt before removing from the heat.\xa0 On each toasted biscuit, layer glazed fried chicken, cheesy fried egg, and pickles (if using), plus additional Chili Crisp Hot Honey if desired. Top with the bun and serve immediately.
\xa0
Want to try this recipe for yourself? Find Chili Crisp Hot Honey Sauce near you by visiting our product page!
']

Name: focaccia-pizza-recipe-with-chili-crisp-hot-honey
Ingredients: ['-- For the Dough --', '4 3/4 cups all-purpose flour', '1 Tbsp. fine sea salt', '2 1/4 tsp. active dry yeast', '2 cups warm water', '1/4 cup olive oil', '1 Tbsp. honey', '-- For The Toppings --', '1 12-oz. jar pizza sauce', '2 cups shredded mozzarella cheese', 'Thick-sliced pepperoni (as desired)', '1 cup whole milk ricotta cheese, seasoned with salt, red pepper flakes, and dried oregano', 'Fresh Basil, chopped for garnish']
Procedure:
['
Make the Dough (12–48 Hours Ahead): Mix flour, salt, and yeast in a large bowl. Add warm water, olive oil, and honey. Stir until fully combined. Cover and let rise at room temp 30 minutes, then fold the dough over itself 4–5 times. Cover and refrigerate 12–48 hours.
', '
Prepare the Pan: Bring dough to room temp for 30 minutes. Grease a 9×13-inch pan with olive oil. Place dough in pan, flip to coat, cover, and let rise until puffy, about 30–45 minutes.
', '
Dimple the Dough: Preheat oven to 425°F. Use oiled fingertips to press dimples all over the dough. Drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey Sauce and gently spread across the surface.
', '
Top the Pizza: Add half the mozzarella, then spread pizza sauce. Add pepperoni and\xa0remaining mozzarella.
', '
Bake and Serve: Bake 25–32 minutes, until golden and bubbling. Cool 10 minutes, then top with dollops of ricotta and drizzle with more Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey before slicing and topping with fresh chopped basil.
\xa0
For more sweet and spicy recipe ideas and info on Chili Crisp Hot Honey, click the link to head to the product page!
']

Name: chicken-and-waffles-recipe-with-chili-crisp-hot-honey
Ingredients: ['1½ lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs or breasts', 'Neutral oil, for frying', '-- For the Dredge --', '1½ cups all-purpose flour', '½ cup cornstarch', '1 teaspoon salt', '1 teaspoon black pepper', '½ teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional for extra heat)', '-- For the Waffles --', '1½ cups all-purpose flour', '2 tablespoons sugar', '1 tablespoon baking powder', '½ teaspoon salt', '1¼ cups milk', '2 large eggs', '4 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted', '1 teaspoon vanilla extract', '-- To Serve --', 'Softened butter', 'Fresh herbs, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey Sauce, for drizzling']
Marinade: ['1 cup buttermilk', '1 teaspoon Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 teaspoon onion powder', '1 teaspoon smoked paprika', '½ teaspoon salt', '½ teaspoon black pepper']
Procedure:
['
Place the chicken in a bowl with buttermilk, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, onion powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat and marinate in the fridge for at least 1 hour (or overnight for best flavor).
', '
In a shallow bowl, mix flour, cornstarch, salt, pepper, and cayenne (if using). Heat 1–2 inches of oil in a heavy-bottomed skillet to 350°F. Remove chicken from marinade, dredge in flour mixture, pressing firmly to coat, then fry in batches for 5–7 minutes per side until golden and crispy. Transfer to a wire rack to drain.
', '
While chicken fries, preheat waffle iron. In a bowl, whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. In another bowl, whisk milk, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla. Combine wet and dry ingredients until just mixed. Cook waffles according to your waffle iron’s instructions until golden and crisp.
', '
To serve, place crispy fried chicken on hot waffles. Drizzle generously with Lee Kum Kee Chili Crisp Hot Honey and top with butter and herbs.
\xa0
Looking for more ways to use Chili Crisp Hot Honey? Visit our product page for details, inspiration, and where to buy!
']

Name: chicken-teriyaki-bowl-recipe-with-spicy-yuzu-dressing
Ingredients: ['1 Tbsp. neutral oil', '1.75 lb. boneless, skinless chicken thighs', '4 cups cooked jasmine rice', '1 cup edamame beans, cooked and shelled', '1 cup shredded red cabbage', '1 cup julienned carrot', '1 tsp. white and black sesame seeds ', 'Sliced green onion, for garnish', 'Sliced fresh red chili (optional)']
Sauce Mix: ['1/4 cup mirin', '1/4 cup cooking sake', '1/4 cup Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. brown sugar', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Yuzu Flavored Sriracha']
Procedure:
['
In a small bowl, combine mirin, cooking sake,\xa0Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, brown sugar, and 1 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Yuzu Flavored Sriracha. Stir until the sugar is dissolved.
', '
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add chicken thighs and cook for 8 minutes, turning once, until browned on both sides.
', '
Pour the Yuzu Sriracha teriyaki sauce into the pan. Cook for 1 minute, flip the chicken, and cook for 1–2 more minutes, or until the sauce is thick, caramelized, and glossy. Remove chicken from pan and slice.
', '
To serve, divide jasmine rice among bowls. Top with edamame, red cabbage, carrot, and sliced chicken. Sprinkle with sesame seeds, sliced red chili and sliced green onion. Finish with a drizzle of additional Lee Kum Kee Yuzu Flavored Sriracha over the top.
\xa0
Click the link for more yuzu sriracha recipes and where to buy the sauce near you!
']

Name: air-fryer-xo-fish-balls-in-honey
Ingredients: ['10 oz. fish paste']
Seasoning: ['3 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce (oil removed)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Procedure:
['
Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.
', '
Take a small portion (around 0.8 oz.) and knead as a ball. Repeat the step until all the mixture are done.
', '
Put the fish balls in the air-fryer. Air-fry them at 392°F for 10 minutes.
', '
Add in honey and toss well. Put it back to air-fryer again. Air-fry for 3 minutes at 356°F.
', '
Tips:
1. When cooking at home, wet your hands with water before you knead. It helps to avoid sticking your hands from the fish paste.

2. Prepare a plate with plastic wrap and brush a layer of oil. Then put the fish balls\xa0in step 2 on the plate. It helps to avoid sticking the plate from the fish balls.
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. baking soda    ', '1 tsp. cornstarch    ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce      ', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['2 oz. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. Chinese rice wine (or white wine)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon ', '1/3 cup water', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
PREPARE THE BROCCOLI. Blanch the broccoli in hot salted water for about 2 minutes or until slightly tender and dark green. Shock it in ice water and drain thoroughly.\xa0\xa0
', '
MARINATE THE BEEF. In a small bowl, whisk together the baking soda, cornstarch, Lee Kum Kee Cooking Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Pour the mixed sauces over your thinly sliced beef flank steak and let it sit for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
\xa0
', '
CREATE THE SAUCE MIX. In a separate small bowl, stir together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Chinese rice wine, Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon, water, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
', '
STIR-FRY. Heat a wok or a large skillet on high heat and add the oil. When a wisp of white smoke appears, toss the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and marinated beef into the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, continually moving the beef slices so they are brown on all sides, but still rare. Once the beef is seared, add the broccoli and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the sauce and keep it all moving.
', '
FINISH AND SERVE. Don’t be afraid to scrape any bits off the bottom of the pan before it starts to burn. Once the sauce has turned into a nice thick glaze, serve immediately with garnishes (optional). Enjoy!
']

Name: spicy-calamari-recipe-with-yuzu-flavored-sriracha
Ingredients: ['1 lb. clean squid with tentacles, bodies cut into 1/3- to 1/2-inch-thick rings', 'Kosher salt, to taste', '1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour', '1/3 cup cornstarch', '1/2 tsp. baking powder', '3/4 tsp. dried oregano', '1/2 tsp. black pepper', '1/2 tsp. cayenne pepper', 'Grape seed oil, for frying', 'Lemon wedges, for serving']
Marinade: ['1/2 cup buttermilk', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Yuzu Flavored Sriracha Sauce']
Procedure:
['
In a medium bowl, whisk together buttermilk and Lee Kum Kee Yuzu Flavored Sriracha Sauce. Add squid rings and tentacles, stir to coat, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
', '
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, baking powder, oregano, black pepper, and cayenne until well combined.
', '
Set a colander over a bowl. Working in batches, remove squid from the marinade, letting excess drip off. Toss in the flour mixture to coat thoroughly, then transfer to the colander. Repeat until all squid is coated.
', '
Preheat oven to 150°F. Line a sheet pan with a wire rack topped with paper towels. In a deep pot, heat 4 inches of grape seed oil to 350–365°F. Test the oil with one ring—if it bubbles and floats, it’s ready.
', '
Fry the squid in batches for 2–3 minutes, or until golden brown and crisp. Transfer to the prepared rack and immediately season with kosher salt. Keep warm in the oven while frying remaining squid. Transfer fried calamari to a serving platter. Squeeze lemon over the top and serve immediately with extra Lee Kum Kee Yuzu Flavored Sriracha on the side for dipping.
\xa0
Click here to get more recipe ideas for yuzu sriracha and find Lee Kum Kee Yuzu\xa0Flavored Sriracha Sauce\xa0near you!
']

Name: yuzu-flavored-buffalo-chicken-wings-recipe
Ingredients: ['2 lbs chicken wings', '1 tablespoon baking powder', '1 teaspoon kosher salt', '½ teaspoon garlic powder', 'Neutral oil spray, for baking', 'Thinly sliced scallions, for garnish', 'Toasted sesame seeds, for garnish', 'Lemon or yuzu wedges, for garnish', 'Ranch dressing, on the side']
Sauce Mix: ['¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Yuzu Flavored Sriracha Sauce', '1 Tbsp. unsalted butter, melted', '1 Tbsp. honey', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '½ tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Procedure:
['
Preheat the oven to 425°F. Pat wings very dry with paper towels. In a large bowl, toss wings with baking powder, salt, and garlic powder until evenly coated.
', '
Place wings on a wire rack set over a foil-lined baking sheet. Spray lightly with oil. Bake for 40 minutes, flipping halfway through, until golden and crispy.
', '
While the wings bake, whisk together Lee Kum Kee Yuzu Flavored Sriracha Sauce, melted butter, honey, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil in a large bowl until smooth.
', '
Transfer hot wings directly into the sauce bowl and toss to coat. Let sit for 2 minutes to absorb the glaze.
', '
Plate the wings and garnish with scallions, sesame seeds, and a squeeze of citrus. Serve immediately with ranch for dipping.
\xa0
Want to try this recipe? Get Lee Kum Kee Yuzu Flavored Sriracha Sauce at the link!
']

Name: pulled-pork-sandwich-recipe-with-hoisin-barbecue-sauce
Ingredients: ['2 lbs. boneless pork shoulder or pork butt', '1 Tbsp. neutral oil (for searing, if using Dutch oven method)', '4 brioche or potato buns, toasted', 'Coleslaw ', 'Pickled red onions or sliced jalapeños ']
Seasoning: ['1 tsp. kosher salt', '1/2 tsp. black pepper', '1/2 tsp. garlic powder', '1/2 tsp. smoked paprika']
Sauce Mix: ['1/2 cup Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Chili Sauce', '2 Tbsp. ketchup', '1 Tbsp. apple cider vinegar', '1 Tbsp. brown sugar', '1 tsp. Dijon mustard']
Procedure:
['
Pat the pork dry and rub with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and smoked paprika.
', '
Cook the Pork using one of the two below methods, based on what is accessible to you:

Slow Cooker Method: Place pork in the slow cooker. Cover and cook on low for 8 hours or high for 4–5 hours, until very tender.
Oven Method: Preheat to 325°F. Heat oil in a Dutch oven or oven-safe pot and sear pork on all sides until browned. Add 1/2 cup water, cover, and roast for 2 hours, or until fork-tender.

', '
While pork cooks, combine\xa0Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce, ketchup, vinegar, brown sugar, and mustard in a saucepan. Simmer over medium heat for 5–7 minutes, stirring often, until glossy and slightly thickened.
', '
Transfer the cooked pork to a large bowl. Shred with two forks. Pour in the hoisin BBQ sauce and toss until fully coated, reserving some of the sauce for serving.\xa0
', '
Spoon sauced pork onto toasted buns. Top with coleslaw and pickled onions or jalapeños. Serve warm with extra sauce on the side.
\xa0
Looking for more ways to cook with hoisin sauce? Check out our recipes page!
']

Name: huli-huli-chicken-pineapple-skewers-recipe
Ingredients: ['2 lbs. boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces', '1 1/2 cups fresh pineapple chunks', 'Skewers (if wooden, soak in water for 30 minutes)', 'Sliced green onions or sesame seeds (optional)']
Seasoning: ['Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo Dressing/Sauce, for drizzling']
Marinade: ['1 cup pineapple juice', '1/2 cup ketchup', '1/2 cup brown sugar', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 tsp. smoked paprika (optional for depth)']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, whisk together pineapple juice, ketchup, brown sugar,\xa0Lee Kum Kee\xa0Hoisin Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce,\xa0Lee Kum Kee\xa0Premium Soy Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, and smoked paprika. Set aside 1/4 cup of the sauce for basting.
', '
Place chicken pieces in a large resealable bag or bowl. Pour remaining sauce over the chicken, toss to coat, and marinate in the fridge for at least 1 hour (up to overnight).
', '
Thread marinated chicken and pineapple chunks onto skewers, alternating for even spacing and flavor.
', '
Preheat the grill to high heat (around 450°F). Grill skewers, turning every minute for 8–10 minutes or until chicken reaches an internal temp of 175°F. In the final 1–2 minutes, brush with reserved sauce and allow to caramelize.\xa0
', '
Let skewers rest for 5–10 minutes. Drizzle with Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo and garnish with green onions or sesame seeds if desired. Serve hot.
\xa0
Check out more flavorful easy chicken recipes on our recipes page!
']

Name: grilled-corn-pasta-salad-recipe-with-chipotle
Ingredients: ['8 oz. rotini or fusilli pasta', '1 Tbsp. olive oil', '2 ears corn, husked', '1 red bell pepper, diced', '1 15 oz. can black beans, drained and rinsed', '2 green onions, sliced', '¼ cup chopped cilantro', 'Crumbled cotija or queso fresco cheese, for topping']
Procedure:
['
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook pasta until al dente according to package instructions. Drain, rinse with cool water, and toss with 1 Tbsp olive oil. Set aside.
', '
Heat a grill or grill pan over medium-high. Grill corn until charred in spots, turning occasionally, about 10 minutes. Let cool slightly, then slice kernels off the cob.
', '
In a small bowl, whisk together Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo, Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, lime juice, smoked paprika, minced chipotle pepper, adobo sauce, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
', '
In a large bowl, combine cooked pasta, grilled corn kernels, diced red bell pepper, black beans, green onions, and cilantro. Pour the dressing over and toss until well coated.
', '
Taste and adjust seasoning. For extra heat, drizzle more Chiu Chow chili crisp oil\xa0on top and finish with crumbled cotija or queso fresco. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
\xa0
Check out more pasta salad recipes on our salad recipes page!\xa0
']

Name: chicken-and-waffle-sliders
Ingredients: ['1 lb. chicken tenders or boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into slider-size pieces', '1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour', '1/2 cup cornstarch', 'Vegetable oil, for frying', '24 mini frozen waffles, toasted according to package instructions', 'Pickles (for serving)']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. garlic powder', '1 Tbsp. smoked paprika', '1 tsp. cayenne pepper (optional, for extra heat)', '1/4 cup Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Chili Sauce (for finishing drizzle)']
Marinade: ['1 cup buttermilk', '3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce ', '1 tsp. salt', '1 tsp. black pepper']
Procedure:
['
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces, buttermilk, Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to coat the chicken thoroughly. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or overnight for maximum flavor.
', '
In a separate shallow dish, combine the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, garlic powder, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper (if using). Mix well.
', '
Heat about 2 inches of vegetable oil in a deep skillet or heavy pot over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F.
', '
Remove the marinated chicken from the fridge. Working one piece at a time, dredge the chicken in the flour mixture, pressing gently to coat evenly. Shake off any excess flour. Carefully place the coated chicken pieces into the hot oil, frying in batches to avoid crowding. Fry until the chicken is golden brown and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes per side, depending on thickness. Transfer the fried chicken to a wire rack or paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
', "
Assemble the Sliders: Place a piece of fried chicken on each mini waffle. Drizzle generously with Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce for a sweet and spicy finish.\xa0Add pickles on top for a tangy crunch, if desired, and cover with another mini waffle to create the slider.
\xa0
If you're looking for more gameday food ideas, check out our appetizers recipes page!
"]

Name: steak-sliders-recipe-with-hot-honey-sauce
Ingredients: ['1 lb flank steak or skirt steak', '1 cup colorful slaw mix ', '1 Tbsp neutral oil (for cooking)', '8 slider buns', '4 slices jack cheese, cut into fours', '1 Tbsp. Butter (for toasting buns)', 'Sliced pickles, for garnish']
Marinade: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['3 Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Chili Sauce', '1/2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 Tbsp. honey', '2 tsp. apple cider vinegar', '1 tsp. Dijon mustard', '1 tsp. red pepper flakes']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, combine 2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Add the steak and toss to coat. Let marinate at room temperature for 10–15 minutes.
', '
Toss shredded slaw mix with Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar and a pinch of salt. Set aside.
', '
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the steak for 2–3 minutes per side until nicely browned. Transfer to a cutting board and let rest for 5 minutes.
', '
In a small saucepan, combine Honey Sriracha Sauce, honey, apple cider vinegar, ½ Tbsp Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Sauce, Dijon mustard, and red pepper flakes. Simmer over low heat for 2–3 minutes until slightly thickened. Brush generously over both sides of the rested steak, then slice thinly against the grain.
', '
Toast slider buns with butter. Place two squares of cheese on the bottom buns, add sliced glazed steak, slaw, and pickles. Top with buns and serve immediately with any leftover hot honey.
\xa0
Give oyster sauce a try in some of our other steak recipes available on the beef recipes page!
']

Name: cheesy-korean-fire-chicken-skillet-recipe
Ingredients: ['1 lb. boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces', '1½ cups shredded low-moisture mozzarella cheese', '2 scallion stalks, sliced, for garnish ', 'White rice and kimchi for serving ']
Marinade: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '2 Tbsp. gochujang (Korean chili paste)', '1 Tbsp. sugar', '½ tsp. black pepper', '⅔ cup water']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, combine the chicken with Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, gochujang, sugar, black pepper, and water. Mix until the chicken is well coated in the thick marinade.
', '
Heat a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and all the sauce. Cook for 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is fully cooked and the sauce has thickened into a glossy coating.
', '
Turn off the heat and evenly sprinkle mozzarella over the chicken. Broil for 2–3 minutes until the cheese is melted, stretchy, and bubbling.
', '
Remove from the broiler and top with sliced scallions.\xa0Serve immediately in the skillet with white rice and kimchi on the side.
\xa0
For more sweet and spicy chicken recipes, check out our chicken recipes page!
']

Name: fried-prawn-with-oyster-flavored-sauce
Ingredients: ['450 g Shrimp, trimmed', '1 Tbsp. Red chili, chopped', '1 Tbsp. Garlic, chopped', '1 Tbsp. Shallots, chopped', '1 Tbsp. Cooking wine', 'Green onion(s), shredded, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['1 tsp. Cooking wine']
Sauce Mix: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Oyster Sauce', '3 Tbsp. Water', '1 Tbsp. Sugar', '1 tsp. Corn starch']
Procedure:
['
Cut open shrimp lightly along the back.
', '
Pan-fry shrimp in 4 Tbsp. oil until golden yellow and done.  Set aside.
', '
Use the remaining oil to saute red chili, garlic and shallot until fragrant.
', '
 Add sauce mix and heat through.
', '
Stir in fried shrimp and cooking wine. Garnish with green onion and serve.
']

Name: hot-honey-crab-rangoon-dip-recipe-by-jasmineandtea
Ingredients: ['2 8 oz. blocks cream cheese, room temperature', '1/2 cup Japanese mayo ', '1 cup (80g) shredded sharp cheddar cheese', '1 cup (80g) shredded mozzarella cheese', 'handful of extra cheese for topping', '2 cup (300g) lump crabmeat', 'wonton wrappers, or crusty bread', 'oil for frying', 'scallions, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce', '2 tsp. granulated sugar', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar', 'Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce, for drizzling']
Procedure:
['
Preheat an oven to 350° Fahrenheit/180° Celsius.\xa0Add the cream cheese, mayo, sugar, Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Rice Vinegar to a mixing bowl.\xa0 Mix well until you get a soft and creamy texture with few lumps. Add in the cheese and mix again, then gently fold in the crab meat. 
', '
Transfer to a baking dish and flatten the top.\xa0 Sprinkle with a small handful of cheese and bake for about 25 to 30 minutes until the top bubbles and is slightly browned.
', '
In the meantime, if making the wonton chips, prepare by cutting the wonton skins into triangles.\xa0Cut the square wontons at each point, then again so you get 4 triangles out of each wonton wrapper.\xa0 
', '
Deep fry at about 375°F/190°C until the wontons turn a light golden brown.\xa0 Make sure to fry only a few at a time since these cook quickly and shake the oil out of each chip with a tong.\xa0 Place the chips on paper towels to soak up excess oil.
', '
Drizzle the finished crab rangoon dip with\xa0Lee Kum Kee Honey Sriracha Sauce, then garnish with scallions.
\xa0
Enjoy and check out our honey sriracha recipes page for more sweet and spicy recipe ideas!

']

Name: rice-cooker-dumpling-noodle-soup-recipe
Ingredients: ['6 cups low-sodium chicken broth', '12–16 frozen dumplings (pork, chicken, or veggie)', '8 oz. ramen, egg, or udon noodles of choice', '1 cup baby spinach or bok choy', '2 green onions, sliced', 'sesame seeds, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce (plus more to serve)', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil (plus more to serve)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic']
Procedure:
['
In the rice cooker bowl, combine chicken broth,\xa0Lee Kum Kee\xa0Premium Soy Sauce,\xa0Lee Kum Kee\xa0Sriracha Chili Sauce,\xa0Lee Kum Kee\xa0Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil,\xa0Lee Kum Kee\xa0Pure Sesame Oil, and\xa0Lee Kum Kee\xa0Minced Garlic.
', '
Stir the broth, then gently add frozen dumplings and noodles into the cooker. Stir lightly to avoid sticking.\xa0Close the lid and set the rice cooker to Cook (or Soup function if available). Let it cook for about 10-15 minutes, or until dumplings and noodles are cooked through and the dumplings float.
', '
In the final 3–5 minutes, stir in spinach or bok choy and green onions. Close the lid and let them wilt gently in the hot broth.\xa0Ladle soup and dumplings into bowls. Top with more sriracha or chili oil and garnish with sesame seeds
\xa0
Enjoy and check out our Lee kum Kee recipes page for more spicy Asian recipes!

']

Name: brothy-rice-recipe-with-spicy-chicken
Ingredients: ['8 oz. boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into bite-size pieces', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '½ cup jasmine rice, rinsed', '3 cups low-sodium chicken broth (warmed)', '½ cup baby bok choy', '¼ cup scallions, sliced', '1 Fresno chili, thinly sliced, for garnish ', '½ lime, cut into wedges, for serving ']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil, plus more for drizzling', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce']
Procedure:
['
In a medium saucepan, heat Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil over medium heat. Add chicken and cook 2–3 minutes until lightly seared. Stir in Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger and cook for 1 minute.
', '
Stir in Lee Kum Kee Chili Garlic Sauce and cook for 30 seconds to release aroma.
', '
Add rinsed jasmine rice and stir to coat. Pour in warm chicken broth and Lee Kum Kee Premium Soy Sauce. Bring to a simmer.
', '
Cover and cook for 15 minutes, stirring once halfway. Add bok choy during the last 2 minutes to wilt.
', '
Spoon into bowls. Top with scallions, Fresno chili slices, and a squeeze of lime. Drizzle extra Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil for a spicy finish. Eat hot and brothy.
\xa0
Chili Crisp Oil and soup is one of our favorite pairings - check out our recipes page for more soups with chili crisp\xa0and other ideas!\xa0
']

Name: air-fryer-xo-fish-balls-in-honey
Ingredients: ['10 oz. fish paste']
Seasoning: ['3 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce (oil removed)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Procedure:
['
Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.
', '
Take a small portion (around 0.8 oz.) and knead as a ball. Repeat the step until all the mixture are done.
', '
Put the fish balls in the air-fryer. Air-fry them at 392°F for 10 minutes.
', '
Add in honey and toss well. Put it back to air-fryer again. Air-fry for 3 minutes at 356°F.
', '
Tips:
1. When cooking at home, wet your hands with water before you knead. It helps to avoid sticking your hands from the fish paste.

2. Prepare a plate with plastic wrap and brush a layer of oil. Then put the fish balls\xa0in step 2 on the plate. It helps to avoid sticking the plate from the fish balls.
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. baking soda    ', '1 tsp. cornstarch    ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce      ', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['2 oz. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. Chinese rice wine (or white wine)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon ', '1/3 cup water', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
PREPARE THE BROCCOLI. Blanch the broccoli in hot salted water for about 2 minutes or until slightly tender and dark green. Shock it in ice water and drain thoroughly.\xa0\xa0
', '
MARINATE THE BEEF. In a small bowl, whisk together the baking soda, cornstarch, Lee Kum Kee Cooking Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Pour the mixed sauces over your thinly sliced beef flank steak and let it sit for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
\xa0
', '
CREATE THE SAUCE MIX. In a separate small bowl, stir together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Chinese rice wine, Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon, water, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
', '
STIR-FRY. Heat a wok or a large skillet on high heat and add the oil. When a wisp of white smoke appears, toss the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and marinated beef into the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, continually moving the beef slices so they are brown on all sides, but still rare. Once the beef is seared, add the broccoli and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the sauce and keep it all moving.
', '
FINISH AND SERVE. Don’t be afraid to scrape any bits off the bottom of the pan before it starts to burn. Once the sauce has turned into a nice thick glaze, serve immediately with garnishes (optional). Enjoy!
']
