Name: miso-sesame-paste
Sauce Mix: ['⅓ cup dry, unroasted peanuts', '3 garlic cloves', '2 packed cups of fresh basil leaves', '¼ cup vegetable oil', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '2 Tbsp. white miso paste', '1 Tbsp. rice vinegar', '2 tsp. fish sauce', '½ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes']
Procedure:
['
Add the peanuts and garlic to a food processor. Pulse until finely chopped.\xa0
', '
Then add in the basil leaves. Pulse until finely chopped.\xa0
', '
Add in the vegetable oil, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, white miso paste, rice vinegar, fish sauce, and crushed red pepper flakes. Blend until smooth and enjoy!
']

Name: larb-gai
Ingredients: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 shallot, sliced', '4 garlic cloves, minced', '2 lbs. ground chicken', 'cilantro leaves, for serving', 'mint leaves, for serving', 'butter lettuce leaves, for serving', 'sliced green onions, for garnish', 'chopped peanuts, for serving']
Seasoning: ['zest and juice from 1 lime', '1 Tbsp. fish sauce', '1 Tbsp. apple cider vinegar', '½ Tbsp. Thai chilies, chopped']
Procedure:
['
Heat a large pan over medium high heat. Pour in the Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil.\xa0
', '
Add in the shallot and garlic. Cook until the garlic is fragrant, about 30 seconds to 1 minute.
', '
Then add in the ground chicken. Cook and crumble the chicken until fully cooked through.
', '
Add in the lime zest and juice, fish sauce, apple cider vinegar, and Thai chilies. Mix it together with the chicken and cook for another 2-3 minutes.\xa0
', '
Serve with cilantro leaves, mint leaves, and butter lettuce. Garnish with sliced green onions and chopped peanuts. Enjoy!
']

Name: ponzu-salmon-wonton-tacos
Ingredients: ['16 wonton wrappers', 'vegetable oil, for frying', '1 lb. salmon filet', '1 ¼ cups shredded cabbage', 'white sesame seeds', 'black sesame seeds', 'sliced green onions', 'lime wedges, for serving']
Seasoning: ['½ tsp. kosher salt', '¼ tsp. black pepper to taste']
Marinade: ['3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Ponzu Plum Grilling and Dipping Sauce ']
Procedure:
['
Fill a pot with 2 inches of vegetable oil. Heat the oil to 350˚F.
', '
Use a ring cutter, about 4 inches in diameter, to cut the wonton wrappers into circles.
', '
Use tongs to fold and hold the wontons into a taco shell shape. Fry them in the oil until they turn golden brown, then place them onto paper towels to drain.\xa0
', '
Preheat the oven to 425˚F. Place the salmon on a baking tray lined with aluminum foil.
', '
Season the salmon with the salt and pepper. Then brush the Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Ponzu Plum Grilling and Dipping Sauce on top. Bake it in the oven for 12-15 minutes until fully cooked. Allow the salmon to cool slightly then use a fork to flake it apart into chunks.\xa0
', '
To assemble the tacos, fill each wonton with salmon and cabbage. Drizzle Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo on top and garnish with white and black sesame seeds and sliced green onions. Serve with lime wedges and enjoy!
']

Name: black-pepper-lentil-burger
Ingredients: ['3 large carrots, 2 peeled, 1 small diced', '1 cup dried lentils, rinsed', '2 ½ cups water', '½ yellow onion, small dice', '¾ cup rolled oats, finely ground', '¾ cup breadcrumbs', 'burger buns, for serving', 'greens, for serving', 'tomatoes, for serving', 'sliced red onions, for serving']
Seasoning: ['½ tsp. salt', '1 tsp. black pepper']
Marinade: ['1 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Black Pepper Grilling and Dipping Sauce']
Sauce Mix: ['1 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce', '3 Tbsp. olive oil', '½ Tbsp. brown sugar', '1 Tbsp. tahini', '¼ tsp. paprika', '½ Tbsp. garlic powder', '¼ tsp. liquid smoke']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, whisk together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce, 2 Tbsp. of olive oil, brown sugar, tahini, paprika, garlic powder, and liquid smoke. Use a vegetable peeler to peel the carrots from end to end, creating wide ribbon-like strips. Add the carrot strips to the sauce and mix until evenly coated. Allow the carrots to marinate in the sauce for about 15 minutes.\xa0
', '
Preheat the oven to 400˚F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and place the carrot strips onto the baking sheet. Bake for 10-15 minutes, flipping them over halfway through. Remove the carrot strips from the oven and set it aside for later.\xa0
', '
Add the lentils, water and salt to a pot. Bring the water to a boil then simmer the lentils for 45 minutes until they are soft and most of the water has evaporated. Set aside for later. Heat a pan over medium heat. Pour in the remaining Tbsp. of olive oil then add in the onion, small diced carrot and black pepper. Cook until the onions turn translucent and the carrots are tender, about 5 minutes.\xa0
', '
Add the lentils, vegetables, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Black Pepper Grilling and Dipping Sauce, oats and breadcrumbs to a bowl. Mix the ingredients together until well combined.
', '
Divide the mixture into 8 evenly-sized portions then shape each portion into a patty. Place the patties onto a parchment lined baking sheet and bake at 400˚F for 15 minutes.
', '
Assemble the burgers with the burger bun, greens, patty, tomato, onion, carrot strips and top bun.\xa0 Enjoy!
']

Name: lemongrass-curry
Ingredients: ['1 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 Tbsp. Thai Red Curry Paste', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Lemongrass Chili Flavored Hoisin Sauce', '3 garlic cloves, thinly sliced', '1 (1-inch) piece ginger, thinly sliced', '2 lemongrass stalks, bottom ⅓ only, tough outer layer removed, bruised with back of knife ', '1 jalapeño pepper, sliced', '1 red chili pepper, sliced', '1 ½ cups sliced red bell peppers', '1 ½ cups sliced orange bell peppers', '1 ½ cups sliced shiitake mushrooms', '1 ½ cup sliced red onion', '2 cups vegetable broth', '1 (13.5 oz.) can of coconut milk', '2 sprigs of basil, plus more for serving', '1 Tbsp. fish sauce', 'sliced green onions, for garnish', 'cooked white rice, for serving', 'lime wedges, for serving']
Procedure:
['
Heat a pot over medium heat and add in the vegetable oil. Mix in the curry paste and Lee Kum Kee Lemongrass Chili Flavored Hoisin Sauce. Cook for about 1 minute.\xa0
', '
Add in the garlic and ginger. Cook for about 2-3 minutes. Then add in the lemongrass, jalapeños, red chili pepper, red bell peppers, orange bell peppers, shiitake mushrooms, and sliced red onion. Cook until the vegetables begin to soften, about 5-8 minutes.
', '
Pour in the vegetable broth and coconut milk and bring it to a simmer, then add in the fish sauce.\xa0
', '
Garnish the curry with additional basil leaves and sliced green onions. Serve with white rice and lime wedges. Enjoy!
']

Name: brown-braised-pork-belly
Ingredients: ['10 oz. pork belly (cut into cubes)', '6–8 slices of ginger']
Marinade: ['2 fl. oz. Lee Kum Kee Brown Braising Sauce  ', '10 fl. oz. water']
Procedure:
['
Heat oil and sauté ginger until fragrant. Add pork belly and stir-fry.
', '
Stir in water and Lee Kum Kee Brown Braising Sauce, cover the pan and cook until it comes to a boil.
', '
Continue to cook for 45 minutes over medium heat until pork belly is tender and fully cooked. Then turn heat to high until sauce thickens. Ready to serve.
']

Name: chiu-chow-chili-oil-popcorn
Ingredients: ['2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '⅓ cup popcorn kernels']
Seasoning: ['1-2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Oil', 'salt, for seasoning']
Procedure:
['
Add the vegetable oil and Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Oil to a large pan. Add in 3 popcorn kernels and cover and heat over medium heat.\xa0
', '
When 1 or 2 kernels pop, add in the remaining popcorn kernels, cover the pan, and shake until the kernels stop popping.
', '
Remove the pan from the heat and season the popcorn with salt. Enjoy!\xa0
']

Name: lomo-saltado-with-aji-sauce
Ingredients: ['2 yukon gold potatoes, cut into matchsticks', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '1 lb. sirloin steak, sliced', '½ red onion, sliced', '2 tomatoes, cut into wedges', '3 stalks green onion, sliced', '½ cup cilantro, chopped']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. olive oil', '¼ tsp. paprika', '¼ tsp. salt', '¼ tsp. garlic powder', 'additional salt and pepper, to taste']
Sauce Mix: ['-----Aji Sauce-----', '½ head iceberg lettuce', '2 garlic cloves', 'juice from half a lemon', '¾ cup chopped cilantro', '1 jalapeño, deseeded, and roughly chopped', '⅓ cup mayo', '-----For Beef-----', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce ', '3 Tbsp. red wine vinegar', '3 tsp. cumin', '⅓ cup beef broth']
Procedure:
['
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CwAWrR3hiew&feature=youtu.be
', '
To make the Aji Sauce, place the iceberg lettuce, garlic cloves, lemon juice, cilantro, jalapeño, and mayo to a food processor. Blend the ingredients together until smooth. Pour the sauce into a bowl and set it aside for later.
', '
Preheat the oven to 450˚F. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and set it aside for later.
', '
Place the potato sticks into a large bowl filled with cold water. Soak the potatoes for 15 minutes. Remove the potatoes from the water and pat them dry with paper towels. Add the potatoes to a large bowl and add olive oil, paprika, salt, and garlic powder. Toss everything together until evenly coated. Spread the potatoes onto the baking sheet and bake them for 20-30 minutes, flipping them halfway through until they are golden brown and tender.
', '
Add the Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce, red wine vinegar, cumin, and beef broth to a bowl. Mix until combined. Heat a pan over high heat and add in the vegetable oil. When the pan is hot, add in the sliced steak. Cook for about 1 minute on each side then remove it from the pan.\xa0
', '
Add the sliced red onion to the pan and cook for about 2 minutes or until it turns translucent. Then add in the tomatoes and prepared sauce mixture. Cook for about 1 minute or until the tomatoes are tender. Add the steak back to the pan and season with salt and pepper to taste. Turn off the heat and add the french fries, sliced green onions and cilantro.\xa0
']

Name: chili-lime-red-curry
Sauce Mix: ['4 Tbsp. red curry paste', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Red Curry Chili Lime Grilling and Dipping Sauce', '2 cups coconut milk', '2 Tbsp. honey', '4 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce ', '2 Tbsp. lime juice', 'pinch of salt']
Procedure:
['
\xa0Heat a medium sized saucepan over medium heat. Add in the curry paste and cook for about 1 minute.\xa0
', '
Mix in the Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Red Curry Chili Lime Grilling and Dipping Sauce, coconut milk, honey, and Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce. Simmer for about 15 minutes or until the sauce reduces by half.
', '
Remove the pan from heat and mix in the lime juice and salt.
', '
Use this sauce to coat any desired foods. Enjoy!
']

Name: chickpea-stuffed-sweet-potatoes
Ingredients: ['3 sweet potatoes, color of your choice scrubbed and cleaned', '15 oz. can chickpeas, drained and rinsed', '1 tsp. avocado oil', 'chopped parsley, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['2 tsp. tandoori masala seasoning', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce ']
Procedure:
['
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
', '
Wrap the sweet potatoes in aluminum foil and place them onto the baking sheet. Bake them for about 2 hours or until tender.
', '
Toss the chickpeas in the avocado oil with tandoori masala seasoning, and Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce. When the sweet potatoes are done, increase oven temperature to 400°F. Add chickpeas to a parchment lined baking sheet and bake for 20-25 minutes.
', '
In a small bowl, mix the Greek yogurt, olive oil, lime juice, salt, and Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic until fully combined.\xa0
', '
To serve, split each sweet potato in half and fluff the insides with a fork. Add some of the chickpeas to each potato and drizzle the yogurt sauce on top. Garnish with fresh parsley. Enjoy!
']

Name: asian-cucumber-salad-2
Ingredients: ['4 cups thinly sliced English cucumber', '¼ cup thinly sliced red onion', '¼ cup finely diced red bell pepper', '3 Tbsp. chopped peanuts', 'cilantro leaves, for garnish']
Procedure:
['
Add the cucumber, red onion, and red bell pepper to a large bowl.\xa0
', '
In a small bowl, whisk together the rice wine vinegar, honey, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, and salt.\xa0
', '
Pour the dressing over the bowl with the cucumbers. Gently toss the vegetables together with the dressing until evenly coated.\xa0
', '
Place the salad in the fridge for at least 1-2 hours to let the flavors marinate.
', '
When ready to serve, top with chopped peanuts, and cilantro leaves.
']

Name: grilled-chicken-teriyaki-rice-bowl-with-asian-cabbage-slaw
Ingredients: ['2 boneless skinless chicken thighs', '½ cup thinly sliced green cabbage', '¼ cup thinly sliced purple cabbage', '½ cup julienne carrots', 'white rice, for serving', '1 avocado, sliced', 'white sesame seeds, for garnish', 'sliced green onions, for garnish', 'cilantro leaves, for garnish']
Marinade: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. honey', '1 Tbsp. ketchup', '2 Tbsp. orange juice', '1 ½ tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic ', '½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger ']
Procedure:
['
Add the Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce, honey, ketchup, orange juice, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger, and chicken thighs to a resealable plastic bag. Massage the chicken together with the marinade for a few seconds then place it in the fridge to marinate for at least 2 hours.
', '
While the chicken marinates, make the cabbage slaw. In a medium sized bowl, add in the rice vinegar, vegetable oil, a pinch of salt, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, and sugar. Whisk until combined. Then add in the green cabbage, purple cabbage, and carrots. Toss until the dressing is evenly distributed. Set it aside for later.
', '
Heat the grill to a high heat. Add the marinated chicken thighs to the grill and cook for about 5-7 minutes on each side.\xa0
', '
Remove the chicken from the grill and allow it to rest for at least 8 minutes before slicing.\xa0
', '
Serve with white rice, avocado, and the cabbage slaw. Garnish with white sesame seeds, sliced green onions and cilantro leaves. Enjoy!
']

Name: air-fryer-xo-fish-balls-in-honey
Ingredients: ['10 oz. fish paste']
Seasoning: ['3 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce (oil removed)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Procedure:
['
Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.
', '
Take a small portion (around 0.8 oz.) and knead as a ball. Repeat the step until all the mixture are done.
', '
Put the fish balls in the air-fryer. Air-fry them at 392°F for 10 minutes.
', '
Add in honey and toss well. Put it back to air-fryer again. Air-fry for 3 minutes at 356°F.
', '
Tips:
1. When cooking at home, wet your hands with water before you knead. It helps to avoid sticking your hands from the fish paste.

2. Prepare a plate with plastic wrap and brush a layer of oil. Then put the fish balls\xa0in step 2 on the plate. It helps to avoid sticking the plate from the fish balls.
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. baking soda    ', '1 tsp. cornstarch    ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce      ', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['2 oz. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. Chinese rice wine (or white wine)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon ', '1/3 cup water', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
PREPARE THE BROCCOLI. Blanch the broccoli in hot salted water for about 2 minutes or until slightly tender and dark green. Shock it in ice water and drain thoroughly.\xa0\xa0
', '
MARINATE THE BEEF. In a small bowl, whisk together the baking soda, cornstarch, Lee Kum Kee Cooking Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Pour the mixed sauces over your thinly sliced beef flank steak and let it sit for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
\xa0
', '
CREATE THE SAUCE MIX. In a separate small bowl, stir together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Chinese rice wine, Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon, water, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
', '
STIR-FRY. Heat a wok or a large skillet on high heat and add the oil. When a wisp of white smoke appears, toss the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and marinated beef into the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, continually moving the beef slices so they are brown on all sides, but still rare. Once the beef is seared, add the broccoli and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the sauce and keep it all moving.
', '
FINISH AND SERVE. Don’t be afraid to scrape any bits off the bottom of the pan before it starts to burn. Once the sauce has turned into a nice thick glaze, serve immediately with garnishes (optional). Enjoy!
']

Name: citrus-panzanella-salad
Ingredients: ['1 grapefruit', '1 orange', '1 ½ cups cubed stale French bread, ½ inch cubes', '1 medium avocado, sliced', '1 shallot, thinly sliced', 'mint leaves, for garnish']
Procedure:
['
In a small bowl, add in the white balsamic vinegar, olive oil, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Triple Citrus Grilling and Dipping Sauce, and a pinch of salt. Whisk until combined and set it aside for later.\xa0
', '
To prepare the citrus, cut off the ends of the grapefruit. Stand it up on one of the flat ends. Cut down the sides of the grapefruit following the curved line where the pith meets the flesh. Continue this process all around the grapefruit. Turn it onto its side and cut ¼ inch rounds. Repeat this process with the orange.
', '
Add the citrus rounds, bread, avocado, and shallot to a large bowl. Drizzle in the prepared dressing and gently toss everything together until combined.\xa0
', '
Transfer to a serving platter and garnish with mint leaves.\xa0
']

Name: lime-cilantro-hoisin-grilled-ribs
Ingredients: ['2 racks (4-5 lbs.) baby back pork ribs, membrane removed', 'cilantro leaves, for garnish', 'white sesame seeds, for garnish', 'lime wedges, for serving']
Marinade: ['1 cup Lee Kum Kee Lime Cilantro Flavored Hoisin Sauce', '½ cup chili sauce', '2 ½ Tbsp. dry sherry', '2 Tbsp. honey', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce ', '1 ½ Tbsp. rice vinegar', '½ tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil ', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic ', '½ tsp. ground ginger']
Procedure:
['
Add the Lee Kum Kee Lime Cilantro Flavored Hoisin Sauce, chili sauce, sherry, honey, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce, rice vinegar, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, and ginger to a bowl. Whisk until combined and set it aside for later.
', '
Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil. Trim any excess fat off of the ribs and cut the racks into 6- to 7-rib sections. Place the rib racks onto the baking sheet.\xa0
', '
Reserve 1 cup of the sauce for later. Pour the remaining sauce over the rib racks, coating both sides. Cover tightly with aluminum foil and refrigerate for four hours to overnight.
', '
Preheat the oven to 300°F. Place the covered ribs in the oven and bake for 1½ hours. Remove the ribs from the oven and remove the foil covering. Coat the ribs with the reserved sauce. Increase the oven temperature to 350˚F. Place the ribs back into the oven, uncovered, and cook until tender and starting to brown, about 30 minutes. Allow the ribs to cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving. Garnish with cilantro and white sesame seeds. Serve with lime wedges. Enjoy!
']

Name: garlic-sesame-kimchi
Ingredients: ['68 fl. oz. water', '2 Tbsp. sea salt', '2 Tbsp. brown sugar', '1 napa cabbage, chopped', '1 daikon, thinly sliced', '2 carrots, thinly sliced', '3 Tbsp. sesame seeds']
Sauce Mix: ['3 Tbsp. Korean chilli flakes ', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce ', '¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1-inch piece of ginger, minced', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic']
Procedure:
['
Add the water to a large pot and bring it to a boil.\xa0 Then pour the hot water into a large bowl and add the salt and one Tbsp. of the brown sugar. Allow the water to cool.
', '
Once the water has cooled, add in the cabbage, daikon, and carrots, and place them in the brine. Place a cover on top of the bowl (using a heavy book, or heavy stable item on top to keep the cover weighted down and the vegetables submerged in the brine. Brine the vegetables for about 12 hours.
', '
To make the paste, mix the chili flakes, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, remaining brown sugar, ginger, and Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic.\xa0
', '
Drain the vegetables from the brine. Using plastic gloves, massage the paste into the vegetables. Mix in the sesame seeds. When everything is combined, transfer the kimchi to sterilized jars. Be sure to pack the kimchi down so there are no air bubbles and the vegetables are submerged in the juice. If not, add a little extra brine to the jar. Top with a lid and Garlic Sesame Kimchi is ready to eat!
']

Name: braised-abalone-and-pork-belly
Ingredients: ['1 lb. pork belly', '2 scallions', '2 Tbsp. Shaoxing wine', '4 sliced ginger', '1 Tbsp. oil', '1/2 cup rock sugar', '1 can of (about 15 eggs) quail eggs (optional)', '1 can of Lee Kum Kee Abalone in Premium Oyster Sauce']
Seasoning: ['1 tsp. salt', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce']
Procedure:
['
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jONGUno2W9g
', '
Add the cubed pork belly, scallions, 1 Tbsp. of Shaoxing wine and sliced ginger into a pot of cold water. Bring to a boil and leave to simmer for 3 minutes. Then drain and discard the ginger and scallions.
', '
Pour oil into a wok on medium-high heat. Add the pork belly to the wok and fry until around ½ cup of pork fat has been rendered from the pork belly (judge based on size of the pork belly). Then remove the pork belly from the wok and set aside.
', '
Add rock sugar to the wok and cook until it is a light brown color. Add the cooked pork belly again. Coat the pork belly thoroughly in the sugar before adding the quail eggs (optional), 1 Tbsp. of Shaoxing wine, salt, Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce, and the scallion stems. Also add the oyster sauce from the Lee Kum Kee Abalone in Premium Oyster Sauce but keep the abalones in the can for now.
', '
After the pork belly and sauce have simmered for 25 minutes, pour in the abalones from the can that have been scored by a knife, along with 1 tsp. of Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce. Then leave to simmer for another 5 minutes.
', '
Garnish with the chopped scallions and another 1 tsp. of Lee Kum Kee XO sauce.
']

Name: sesame-oil-chicken
Ingredients: ['2 lbs. chicken thighs, skin-on', '1 ½ cup rice wine + 2 Tbsp. for soaking the goji berry', '1 oz. dried goji berry', '1 tsp. oil ', '1 pc. ginger, sliced']
Seasoning: ['3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Pure Sesame Oil ', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Bouillon Powder Flavored with Chicken']
Procedure:
['
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Xw4aYbiZQUk
', '
Prepare the chicken for cooking. Add 2 Tbsp. of rice wine over the dried goji berries and set aside to soak.
', '
Take a soup pot and bring to a medium-high heat. Add the chicken (skin side down), stir-fry some ginger slices in oil for 1 minute.
', '
Once the chicken is sizzling and the ginger slices are slightly brown on the edges, reduce the heat to medium and add 2 Tbsp. of Lee Kum Kee Premium Pure Sesame Oil.\xa0
', '
After 2-3 minutes – or when the surface of the chicken is slightly brown – pour in 1 ½ cup of rice wine. If the chicken is not fully submerged, add water. Bring the pot to a boil and leave to simmer for 25 minutes with the lid on.
', '
Add the soaked goji berries and any remaining rice wine in the bowl. Also add in another 1 Tbsp. of Lee Kum Kee Premium Pure Sesame Oil, and Lee Kum Kee Premium Bouillon Powder Flavored with Chicken. Place the lid on the pot and leave to simmer for another 5 minutes at low heat. Serve while hot.
']

Name: poached-fish-with-chili-oil-and-herbs
Ingredients: ['1 skinless, boneless fish fillet (preferably black cod)', '4 Tbsp. canola oil (divided)', '1 Tbsp. green Sichuan peppercorns', '5 pcs. dried chilies ', '1/2 cup Chinese celery (either celery and bean sprouts as an alternative)', '3 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '3 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '6 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Oil', '2 Tbsp. doubanjiang ', '1 sprig Sichuan peppercorns ', '1 quart chicken stock', '1 Tbsp. celery leaves ', '1 Tbsp. cilantro ', '1 Tbsp. scallions', '1 cup, cooked white rice for serving ']
Marinade: ['2 tsp. Shaoxing cooking wine or dry sherry wine  ', '1/2 tsp. salt', '1/2 tsp. white pepper ', '2 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HONUpWxoskg
', '
Slice the fish fillet diagonally. Marinate with Shaoxing cooking wine, salt, white pepper, and corn starch.
', '
In a wok, add green Sichuan peppercorns and dried chilies in 1 Tbsp. oil over a low heat until fragrant (do not burn them). Mortar when cooled, then set aside.
', '
In the same wok, heat up 1 Tbsp. oil over a medium heat, stir in celery. Cook for 1.5 minutes or so (30 seconds if using bean sprouts instead). Transfer to a serving bowl. Set aside.
', '
Sear the fish in about 2 Tbsp. oil on both sides for about a minute and a half.
', '
Heat up Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Oil, Doubanjiang, Sichuan peppercorn and dried chili mixture, followed by the chicken stock. Once it gets to a simmer, add in the celery and poached fish, then let cook for 1 minute. Serve by placing the fish and broth in a serving bowl, garnished with celery leaves, cilantro, scallions, Sichuan peppercorns and a side of white rice.
']

Name: grilled-char-siu-chicken
Ingredients: ['1 whole chicken (3-4 lbs.)', '1 Tbsp. scallions for garnish', '1 Tbsp. micro cilantro for garnish']
Marinade: ['1 cup sugar ', '2 tsp. 5 spice powder', '2 Tbsp. beet powder ', '½ cup honey ', '¾ cup Shaoxing wine or dry sherry wine ', '½ cup Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger ', '⅓ cup Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '½ cup Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce ', '1 ⅓ cup Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce', '1 ¼ cup Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce ', '2 Tbsp. canola oil']
Procedure:
['
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1bwZZPadcc
', '
In a high speed blender, blend all the marinade ingredients.
', '
Place 1 whole chicken (3 to 4 pounds), breast side down, on a work surface. Starting at the thigh end, cut along one side of the backbone with kitchen shears. Turn chicken around; cut along the other side. Discard or save backbone for stock. Flip chicken over and open it like a book. Press firmly on the breastbone to flatten. If you had the chicken spatchcocked by the butcher then you can omit the spatchcocking process.
', '
Marinate the spatchcocked* chicken for at least 4 hours, remove from the fridge and let sit for an hour.
', '
Heat grill or the oven up to 350°F cook for 40 minutes or until the inside of the chicken reaches 160°F. Remove from oven, cut in half along the breast bone, garnish with scallions and micro cilantro. Serve and enjoy!\xa0
']

Name: pork-katsu-sandwiches-with-chili-miso-aioli
Ingredients: ['4 cups grapeseed oil (for frying)', '6 pc pork loin, thinly pounded out', '2 cups flour', '6 eggs, whisked until smooth ', '3 cups panko', '3 cups finely shredded iceberg lettuce', '3 cups finely shredded cabbage', '12 slices soft Japanese milk bread or any available white sandwich bread']
Seasoning: ['2 Tbsp. kosher salt + more to taste', '1 Tbsp. ground white pepper']
Procedure:
['
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hk3pdOw8zKo
', '
In a high speed blender, blend the chili crisp from the whole bottle of Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Oil with shiro miso, take all contents out into a bowl. Then whisk in the Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo to finish the aioli.
', '
Fill large skillet with oil for frying then season pounded pork loin with salt and pepper. Place flour on a plate. Beat eggs and place in a shallow baking dish. Place panko in a shallow baking dish. Work with 1 pork loin at a time, dredge in flour, shake off excess, then dip into egg mixture, turn to coat evenly; carefully coat with panko, pressing panko gently to adhere to pork.
', '
Heat oil in a large cast iron skillet (pan should be large enough to hold 2 pork loins) over medium-high heat.\xa0 Add 2 loins to the skillet and cook until golden brown on both sides, 8-10 minutes. Transfer to a baking rack to allow excess oil to drain. Repeat with remaining pork loins.\xa0
', '
Mix together the shredded iceberg lettuce and cabbage. Spread aioli mixture onto the white bread, followed by cabbage and lettuce, and pork cutlets. Cut off the crust for clean presentation, serve and enjoy!\xa0
']

Name: plum-hoisin-bbq-roasted-flank-steak
Ingredients: ['3 lb. flank steak ', '2 Tbsp. canola oil ']
Seasoning: ['salt to taste']
Marinade: ['6 ripe Santa Rosa plums or any available plums + more for garnish', '1/4 cup packed light brown sugar ', '1/8 cup shaoxing wine or dry sherry wine ', '2 Tbsp. freshly squeezed orange juice ', '1 Tbsp. dry mustard or wasabi', '2 tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '3 Tbsp. peeled and grated fresh ginger', '2 scallions, white and green parts, thinly sliced based on preference, plus more for garnish', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1/2 cup Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', '1/4 cup Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce']
Procedure:
['
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HaZf8n3Xuag
', '
Mix together the marinade ingredients, then arrange meat in a shallow nonreactive dish or re-sealable plastic bag. Add ½ of the marinade; rub gently into meat. Cover; refrigerate for 5 hours, turning meat occasionally.
', '
Before cooking, remove the meat from the refrigerator and let it come to room temperature.\xa0
', '
Preheat pan with 2 Tbsp. canola oil, add the steak to the pan and grill for 3-5 minutes on each side, place in the oven for the last 5 minutes.\xa0
', '
Remove from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes, then add the glazed marinade to the meat, slice against the grain and add salt between the slices if desired.\xa0
', '
Garnish with plums and scallions, serve and enjoy.\xa0
']

Name: zaru-soba
Ingredients: ['14 oz. dried soba noodles', 'shredded nori, for garnish', 'sliced green onions, for garnish']
Procedure:
['
Add the sake to a small pot and bring it to a boil. Allow it to boil for a few seconds to let the alcohol evaporate.\xa0
', '
Then add in the mirin, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Dipping Soy Sauce, kombu, and bonito flakes. Bring the mixture to a boil then lower the heat to simmer. Simmer the sauce for 5 minutes, then remove it from the heat. Let the sauce cool completely then strain and set aside for later.\xa0
', '
Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add in the dried soba noodles and cook them according to the package instructions. Drain the soba and rinse it under cold water until they are chilled. Drain any excess water.
', '
Portion the soba and garnish with shredded nori and sliced green onions. Serve each portion with a cup of the dipping sauce on the side. Enjoy!
']

Name: red-braised-meat-stew
Ingredients: ['1 tsp. oil ', '24 oz. pork belly (cut into 1-inch pieces) ', '5 oz. onion (sliced) ', '5 oz. carrot (diced) ', '5 oz. potato (diced) ', '1 oz. green onion (sliced) ', '1 ½ cup water ', 'some cornstarch for thickening if needed']
Seasoning: ['1 pack of Lee Kum Kee Slow Cook Sauce for Red Braised Meat ', '3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger ', '3 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic ']
Procedure:
['
Heat 1 tsp. oil in a pan and pan-fry the diced pork until browned.\xa0
', '
Place into a slow cooker (if using) with all remaining ingredients and seasonings. Season to taste.\xa0
', '
Cook 4-6 hours on high or 8 hours on low.\xa0
', '
Add more water as needed. Add a little corn starch to thicken as required.\xa0
', '
Serve with steamed vegetables, rice, noodles or mashed potatoes.\xa0
']

Name: pad-thai-noodles
Ingredients: ['oil, for heating pan', '7 oz. pork ', '7 oz.  prawns ', '10 ½ oz. dry flat rice noodles (cooked)', 'mixed vegetables (sliced): carrot, cabbage, bean sprouts, 2 spring onions', '3 eggs, scrambled ', '2 red chilies (finely chopped, to taste)', 'crushed peanuts, cilantro and lime wedges (for garnish) ']
Seasoning: ['1 pack of Lee Kum Kee Ready Sauce for Pad Thai ']
Procedure:
['
Heat a little oil in a pan on medium heat. Stir-fry pork and prawns for 5 minutes.
', '
Combine the cooked noodles with Lee Kum Kee Ready Sauce for Pad Thai, vegetables, scrambled eggs and chilies. Mix thoroughly.\xa0
', '
Garnish with crushed peanuts, cilantro and lime wedges and serve.\xa0
']

Name: air-fryer-xo-fish-balls-in-honey
Ingredients: ['10 oz. fish paste']
Seasoning: ['3 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce (oil removed)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Procedure:
['
Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.
', '
Take a small portion (around 0.8 oz.) and knead as a ball. Repeat the step until all the mixture are done.
', '
Put the fish balls in the air-fryer. Air-fry them at 392°F for 10 minutes.
', '
Add in honey and toss well. Put it back to air-fryer again. Air-fry for 3 minutes at 356°F.
', '
Tips:
1. When cooking at home, wet your hands with water before you knead. It helps to avoid sticking your hands from the fish paste.

2. Prepare a plate with plastic wrap and brush a layer of oil. Then put the fish balls\xa0in step 2 on the plate. It helps to avoid sticking the plate from the fish balls.
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. baking soda    ', '1 tsp. cornstarch    ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce      ', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['2 oz. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. Chinese rice wine (or white wine)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon ', '1/3 cup water', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
PREPARE THE BROCCOLI. Blanch the broccoli in hot salted water for about 2 minutes or until slightly tender and dark green. Shock it in ice water and drain thoroughly.\xa0\xa0
', '
MARINATE THE BEEF. In a small bowl, whisk together the baking soda, cornstarch, Lee Kum Kee Cooking Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Pour the mixed sauces over your thinly sliced beef flank steak and let it sit for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
\xa0
', '
CREATE THE SAUCE MIX. In a separate small bowl, stir together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Chinese rice wine, Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon, water, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
', '
STIR-FRY. Heat a wok or a large skillet on high heat and add the oil. When a wisp of white smoke appears, toss the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and marinated beef into the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, continually moving the beef slices so they are brown on all sides, but still rare. Once the beef is seared, add the broccoli and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the sauce and keep it all moving.
', '
FINISH AND SERVE. Don’t be afraid to scrape any bits off the bottom of the pan before it starts to burn. Once the sauce has turned into a nice thick glaze, serve immediately with garnishes (optional). Enjoy!
']

Name: singapore-noodles
Ingredients: ['9 oz. rice noodles/ vermicelli', '2 Tbsp. oil for cooking ', '9 oz. prawns, de-veined & drained ', '5 oz. onion, diced ', '9 oz. barbecue pork /ham, chopped ', '7 oz. carrot, diced ', '5 oz. bell peppers, diced ', '2 beaten & fried eggs, shredded (optional) ']
Seasoning: ['1 pack of Lee Kum Kee Ready Sauce for Singapore Noodles ']
Procedure:
['
Cook rice noodles in boiling water according to packet instructions or until tender. Drain.\xa0
', '
Heat 2 Tbsp. of oil in a wok and stir fry prawns and onion until cooked. Add roasted barbecue pork, carrot, bell peppers and stir fry for 2 minutes.\xa0
', '
Add rice noodles and 1 pack of Lee Kum Kee Ready Sauce for Singapore Noodles. Stir well and heat through. Sprinkle with shredded egg.
']

Name: ratatouille
Ingredients: ['1 small eggplant, sliced ⅛ inch thick', '1 zucchini, sliced ⅛ inch thick', '3 roma tomatoes, sliced ⅛ inch thick', '1 tsp. parsley, minced for garnish']
Sauce Mix: ['3 Tbsp. olive oil', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '½ yellow onion, chopped', '½ cup diced red bell pepper', '14 oz. crushed tomatoes', '1 tsp. dried basil', '¼ tsp. dried thyme leaves', '2 tsp. dried parsley', 'salt and pepper, to taste']
Procedure:
['
https://www.youtube.com/watch?app=desktop&v=W20n29cc9pg&feature=youtu.be
', '
Preheat the oven to 375˚F. Heat a pan over medium heat. Add 2 Tbsp. of the olive oil to the pan then add in the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, onion, and red bell pepper.
', '
Cook the vegetables for 3-4 minutes or until they become tender. Pour in the crushed tomatoes, dried basil, dried thyme, and dried parsley. Mix until combined. Simmer for 15 minutes until the sauce has slightly thickened. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
', '
Pour the sauce into an 8” x 8” baking dish. Form small stacks of the sliced vegetables. Turn the stacks sideways then arrange them into the pan so that the slices are standing up.
', '
Brush the remaining Tbsp. of olive oil on top. Cover the pan with foil and bake it in the oven for 30 minutes.\xa0
', '
Remove the foil covering then bake it for an additional 15 minutes or until the vegetables are tender. Allow the ratatouille to cool slightly before serving, top with minced parsley. Serve and enjoy!
']

Name: dak-galbi-korean-spicy-chicken-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1” cubes', '6 oz. rice cake sticks', '3 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '½ sweet potato, batonnet', '½ carrot, peeled and sliced on a bias', 'white sesame seeds, for garnish', 'sliced green onions, for garnish']
Marinade: ['3 Tbsp. gochujang', '2 Tbsp. rice wine', '1 Tbsp. gochugaru (or red pepper flakes)', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce', '1 Tbsp. raw sugar', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '1 tsp. Korean curry powder', '½ onion, minced']
Procedure:
['
Add the gochujang, rice wine, gochugaru, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce, raw sugar, Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic, Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger, Korean curry powder, and onion to a large bowl. Mix the ingredients together until combined then mix in the chicken thighs. Let the chicken marinate for at least 30 minutes.
', '
While the chicken marinates, soak the rice cakes in warm water for about 10 minutes.\xa0
', '
Heat a large pan over medium high heat. Pour in the vegetable oil.
', '
Add in the rice cakes, sweet potato, carrot, and marinated chicken. Cook on medium high heat for 3-4 minutes then lower the heat to medium low. Cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through.
', '
Remove the pan from heat. Garnish with white sesame seeds and sliced green onions and enjoy!
']

Name: crispy-sesame-tofu
Ingredients: ['1 block extra firm tofu, drained and diced into ½-inch cubes', 'cooking spray', '2 tsp. cornstarch', '2 Tbsp. white sesame seeds', 'sliced green onions, for garnish']
Sauce Mix: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce', '1 tsp. red pepper flakes', '4 Tbsp. honey', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', 'salt and pepper to taste', '1 Tbsp. rice wine vinegar', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Procedure:
['
Preheat the oven to 375˚F. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spray it with cooking spray.
', '
In a medium bowl, combine 1 Tbsp. of the Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce, red pepper flakes, 3 Tbsp. of the honey, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce, salt and pepper to taste, rice wine vinegar, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Mix until combined.\xa0
', '
Add the diced tofu to the sauce mix and let it marinate for 15 minutes. Place the tofu onto the prepared baking sheet and spray the top with cooking spray. Reserve the leftover sauce mix.
', '
Bake the tofu in the oven for 20 minutes. Flip the tofu onto the other side and bake for an additional 20 minutes until golden brown and crispy.\xa0
', '
Mix the remaining Tbsp. of Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce and honey and cornstarch into the leftover sauce. Heat a saucepan over medium high heat then add in the sauce and tofu. Bring the sauce to a boil then lower the heat so that it is simmering and the sauce thickens.\xa0
', '
Remove the pan from heat. Mix in the white sesame seeds and garnish with sliced green onions. Enjoy!
']

Name: braided-sesame-egg-bread
Ingredients: ['1 Tbsp. active dry yeast', '½ cup warm water', '½ cup granulated sugar', '1 cup milk, warmed', '¾ cup unsalted butter, melted', '¼ cup, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '6 large eggs', '1 ½ tsp. salt', '6 ½ cups all-purpose flour', 'cooking spray, as needed', '2 Tbsp. white sesame seeds']
Procedure:
['
Add the yeast, water, and 2 tsp. of the sugar to a large bowl. Allow the mixture to stand until the yeast activates and it turns foamy.
', '
Add in the remaining sugar, milk, butter, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, 5 of the eggs, salt, and 6 cups of the flour. Mix the dough and when it begins to form a ball, knead it together with your hands. If the dough is too sticky, sprinkle in the remaining flour a little at a time until the dough is tacky but does not stick to your hands. Knead for about 6-7 minutes.
', '
Spray a large bowl with cooking spray then place the dough inside. Spray the top of the dough with cooking spray then cover with plastic wrap. Allow the dough to double in size in a warm area for about 40 minutes to an hour.\xa0
', '
Punch the dough down to release any air then divide it in half. Cut each half into 3 evenly sized pieces and roll them out until they form a 12-inch long rope. Braid the 3 strands together and repeat with the remaining 3 strands of dough.
', '
Preheat the oven to 350˚F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Place the braided loaves onto the sheet tray. Spray cooking spray on top of them and cover with plastic wrap. Allow the loaves to double in size, about 30 minutes.
', '
Beat the remaining egg in a small bowl. Remove the plastic wrap from the top of the loaves. Use a pastry brush to coat the tops of the loaves with the egg. Then sprinkle the white sesame seeds on top. Bake the loaves in the oven for about 30 minutes until they have turned golden brown and are fully cooked through. Enjoy!
']

Name: grilled-hawaiian-bbq-chicken
Ingredients: ['1 lb. chicken tenderloins', '8 oz. canned pineapple rings']
Marinade: ['½ cup Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce', '¼ cup water', '¼ cup brown sugar', '1 garlic clove, minced', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '½ cup coconut milk', '¼ cup pineapple juice']
Procedure:
['
In a large bowl, combine the Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce, water, brown sugar, garlic, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, coconut milk, and pineapple. Mix with a whisk until combined and the sugar has dissolved.
', '
Add the chicken tenderloins to a reseal-able plastic bag then pour the marinade inside. Massage the chicken with the marinade for a few seconds to ensure that the chicken is evenly coated. Place the bag into the fridge to marinate for at least 2 hours.\xa0
', '
Heat the grill over a medium heat. Remove the chicken tenderloins from the marinade and place them onto the grill. Cook the chicken for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until they are fully cooked through. Remove them from the grill.
', '
Add the pineapple rings to the grill and cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side. Remove them from the grill and serve with the grilled chicken. Enjoy!
']

Name: grilled-lamb-chops-with-chimichurri
Ingredients: ['6 lamb chops']
Seasoning: ['salt and pepper to taste']
Marinade: ['1 cup Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Black Pepper Grilling Sauce']
Procedure:
['
Add the lamb chops and Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Black Pepper Grilling Sauce to a resealable plastic bag. Massage the lamb chops together with the sauce so that they are evenly coated. Place it in the fridge to marinate for about 4 hours.
', '
Add the cilantro, parsley, mint, oregano, and garlic to a food processor. Pulse until the ingredients are roughly chopped. Then mix in the red wine vinegar, olive oil, lemon juice, red pepper flakes, and salt and pepper to taste. Set the chimichurri aside for later.
', '
Remove the lamb chops from the bag and season both sides with salt and pepper.\xa0
', '
Preheat the grill to medium high heat. Add the lamb chops to the grill and cook for about 2-3- minutes on each side.
', '
Allow the lamb chops to rest for 5-10 minutes before serving with the chimichurri. Enjoy!
']

Name: lemon-pepper-shrimp-scampi
Ingredients: ['8 oz. fettuccine pasta', '1 Tbsp. olive oil', '6 garlic cloves, minced', '¼ cup unsalted butter, diced', '1 lb. shrimp, peeled and deveined', 'chopped parsley for garnish', '¼ lemon, thinly sliced for garnish']
Sauce Mix: ['½ cup chicken broth', '¼ cup white wine', 'juice from 1 lemon', '½ tsp. lemon zest', '1 ½ tsp. ground black pepper', 'salt to taste', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Lemon Pepper Grilling and Dipping Sauce']
Procedure:
['
Cook the fettuccine according to the package directions. Drain the pasta when it has finished cooking.
', '
While the pasta is cooking, heat a large pan over medium high heat. Add in the olive oil and then the garlic. Sauté the garlic for about 1 minute.
', '
Add the chicken broth, white wine, lemon juice, lemon zest, pepper, salt, and Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Lemon Pepper Grilling and Dipping Sauce to the pan. Mix until combined and simmer until the sauce reduces by half.
', '
When the sauce has reduced, add in the butter and allow it to melt. Mix it into the sauce then add in the shrimp. Cook until opaque and fully cooked through. Add in the pasta and toss everything together. Adjust seasoning if necessary. Garnish with chopped parsley and lemon slices if desired and enjoy!
']

Name: pork-katsu-sando
Ingredients: ['2 slices of pork loin', 'vegetable oil, for frying', '⅓ cup all-purpose flour', '1 large egg, beaten', '1 cup panko', '4 slices Japanese milk bread (or white bread)', '1 cup, finely shredded green cabbage']
Seasoning: ['salt and pepper to taste']
Sauce Mix: ['1 Tbsp. margarine', '1 Tbsp. mayo', '½ tsp. mustard', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce', '1 Tbsp. tonkatsu sauce', '1 Tbsp. ketchup']
Procedure:
['
Combine the margarine, mayo, and mustard together in a small bowl. Set it aside for later. In a separate bowl, combine the Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce, tonkatsu sauce, and ketchup. Set it aside for later.
', '
Place the pork slices onto a cutting board and pound them with a meat mallet until they have tenderized and are about ½ an inch thick. Shape and trim the pork so that it fits the shape and size of the bread.\xa0 Season both sides with salt and pepper.\xa0
', '
Pour the vegetable oil into a pan with sides so that it is about ½ an inch deep. Heat over medium heat.
', '
Pour the flour, egg, and panko each into their own separate shallow dishes. Dredge each slice in flour, dusting off any excess. Then dip them into the egg, making sure that the flour has been absorbed.\xa0
', '
Add the pork to the hot oil and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side. When the pork is fully cooked, remove them from the pan and drain them on paper towels.\xa0
', '
To assemble the sandwich, spread some of the mayo mixture onto one slice of bread. Add half of the shredded cabbage on top. Drizzle some of the tonkatsu sauce mixture on top of the cabbage then lay the pork on top. Drizzle more of the tonkatsu sauce on top of the pork. Top it with another slice of bread then cut it in half. Repeat this process to assemble the remaining sandwich. Enjoy!
']

Name: peppercorn-shredded-chicken-with-seasoning-soy-sauce
Ingredients: ['2 chicken drumsticks (approx. 1 lb.)', '1 oz. green bell pepper', '1 oz. red bell pepper', '1 oz. onion', 'cilantro (optional)']
Sauce Mix: ['5 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sodium Reduced Seasoning Soy Sauce', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Premium Pure Sesame Oil', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Sichuan Peppercorn Flavored Oil', '½ oz. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '½ oz. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic']
Procedure:
['
Cook the chicken. Drain under cold water, then shred the chicken.
', '
Blanch the green and red peppers and onion. Add cilantro (optional), and combine the sauce mix ingredients.
', '
Drizzle the sauce mix on top or on the side for dipping.
']

Name: drunken-shrimp-with-seasoning-soy-sauce
Ingredients: ['½ lb. shrimp (with head on)', '½ oz. green bell pepper', '½ oz. red bell pepper', '½ oz. scallions']
Marinade: ['10 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sodium Reduced Seasoning Soy Sauce', '3 Tbsp. Huadiao wine', '½ oz. Lee Kum Kee Minced Ginger', '½ oz. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic']
Procedure:
['
Clean the shrimp, then blanch until cooked. Drain under cold water and set aside.
', '
Add all marinade ingredients to the shrimp.\xa0
', '
Soak for one hour (or longer for more flavor), then serve.
']

Name: sesame-soy-roasted-peanuts
Ingredients: ['2 cups raw peanuts', '2 Tbsp. white sesame seeds']
Sauce Mix: ['½ cup maple syrup', '2 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce ', 'salt, 1 pinch ']
Procedure:
['
https://www.youtube.com/watch?app=desktop&v=-vO-wT4u8Ig&feature=youtu.be
', '
Place peanuts on a baking tray and bake for 8-10 minutes at 325°F.\xa0
', '
In a mixing bowl add maple syrup, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce, salt, and mix. Remove the peanuts from the oven toss into sauce mixture until coated.\xa0
', '
Place coated peanuts back onto baking tray and top with white sesame seeds. Bake for an additional 15-18 minutes, let cool then serve and enjoy!\xa0
']

Procedure:

Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.

Name: tempura-dipping-sauce
Ingredients: ['1 cup water', '1 Tbsp. dashi granules', '¼ cup mirin', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Dipping Soy Sauce', 'sliced green onions, for garnish', 'white sesame seeds, for garnish']
Procedure:
['
Pour the water into a small pot and bring it to a boil. Mix in the dashi granules and cook for 2 minutes.
', '
Remove the pot from the heat then mix in the mirin and Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Dipping Soy Sauce.\xa0
', '
Garnish with sliced green onions and white sesame seeds. Enjoy with deep fried tempura of your choice!
']

Name: spicy-chimichurri-steak
Ingredients: ['2 lbs. flank steak', 'additional parsley leaves, for garnish', 'sliced green onions, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['additional salt and pepper, to taste']
Sauce Mix: ['1 shallot, cut into small chunks', '¾ cup fresh parsley leaves', '1 Tbsp. fresh oregano leaves', '2 garlic cloves', '1 ½ tsp. kosher salt', '1 tsp. black pepper', '½ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', '½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce', '¾ cup olive oil', '½ cup red wine vinegar']
Procedure:
['
Place the shallot, parsley leaves, oregano leaves, garlic, salt, and pepper in a food processor. Pulse the ingredients together until finely chopped. Transfer the mixture into a bowl then mix in the red pepper flakes, Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce, olive oil, and red wine vinegar. Set the chimichurri sauce aside for later.\xa0
', '
Pat the steak dry with paper towels. Season both sides with salt and pepper.\xa0
', '
Preheat the grill to a medium high heat. Place the steak on the grill and cook for about 6 minutes on each side for a medium rare or cook the steak until it reaches your desired level of doneness.
', '
Remove the steak from the grill and allow it to rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
', '
Serve with the chimichurri sauce and garnish with additional parsley leaves and sliced green onions. Enjoy!
']

Name: korean-inspired-bulgogi-tacos
Ingredients: ['2 tsp. vegetable oil', '2 garlic cloves, minced', '8 oz. thinly sliced beef', '6 flour taco tortillas', '1 ½ cups shredded cabbage slaw mix', '1 ½ cups kimchi', 'sliced green onions, for garnish', 'black sesame seeds, for garnish', 'white sesame seeds, for garnish', 'lime wedges, for serving']
Sauce Mix: ['2 Tbsp. brown sugar', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Sweet Soy Sauce', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil', '1 tsp. crushed red pepper flakes']
Procedure:
['
In a small bowl, mix the brown sugar, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Sweet Soy Sauce, Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil, and red crushed pepper flakes together.\xa0
', '
Heat a pan over medium high heat. Pour in the vegetable oil, then add in the garlic. Stir until fragrant, about 1 minute.\xa0
', '
Add in the sliced beef and cook until they have browned nicely. Pour in the sauce mixture and stir it together with the beef. Cook for about 2 minutes then remove the pan from heat and set it aside for later.
', '
If you would like to char the tortillas, turn on the stovetop so that there is a small flame.\xa0 Place a tortilla directly onto the stovetop above the flame and cook on both sides until it reaches your desired level of doneness. Repeat this process with the remaining tortillas.
', '
To serve, add some of the bulgogi, cabbage slaw, and kimchi to a tortilla. Garnish with sliced green onions, black sesame seeds, and white sesame seeds. Serve with a lime wedge on the side. Enjoy!
']

Name: grilled-scallops-with-coconut-rice-and-mango-salsa
Ingredients: ['20 large sea scallops', '3 cups jasmine rice', '2 ¼ cups water', 'lime wedges, for serving', 'sliced radishes, for garnish', 'cilantro leaves, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['20 oz. canned coconut milk ', '1 ½ tsp. granulated sugar', '1 ½ tsp. kosher salt']
Marinade: ['1 cup Lee Kum Kee Lemongrass Chili Flavored Hoisin Sauce']
Sauce Mix: ['1 lime, juiced', '1 tsp. ginger powder', '½ tsp. chili powder', '1 small mango, diced', '1 red bell pepper, diced', '1 red chili pepper, minced', '1 jalapeño, minced', '1 shallot, minced']
Procedure:
['
Add the scallops and Lee Kum Kee Lemongrass Chili Flavored Hoisin Sauce to a bowl. Mix until the scallops are evenly coated with the sauce. Place in the fridge for at least 1 hour to marinate.
', '
While the scallops marinate, whisk the lime juice, ginger powder, and chili powder together in a large bowl until the spices are evenly mixed. Then add in the mango, red bell pepper, red chili pepper, jalapeño, and shallot. Mix until combined then set it aside in the fridge.
', '
Rinse and drain the jasmine rice a few times with cold water until the water runs clear.\xa0
', '
Add the rinsed rice, coconut milk, water, sugar, and salt to a pot. Bring the mixture to a boil then lower the heat so that it is simmering. Cover the pot with a lid and simmer for 20 minutes. Uncover the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes. Fluff the rice with a fork when it is done.
', '
Heat the grill to a medium high heat. Place the scallops on the grill and cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side.\xa0
', '
Serve the grilled scallops with the coconut rice, mango salsa, and lime wedges. Garnish with radish slices and cilantro leaves. Enjoy!
']

Name: crab-linguine
Ingredients: ['10 oz. linguine', 'pinch of salt', '2 Tbsp. extra virgin olive oil', '4 garlic cloves, minced', '¼ cup dry white wine', '2 cups cherry tomatoes', '3 Tbsp. unsalted butter', '8 oz. lump crab meat', 'lemon wedges, for serving', 'chopped parsley, for garnish', 'lemon zest, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '¼ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes', 'salt and pepper, to taste', '1 Tbsp. lemon juice']
Procedure:
['
Bring a pot of water to a boil then sprinkle in some salt. Add the linguine to the boiling water and cook for about 7 minutes or until it is al dente. Drain the pasta and rinse with cold water. Set it aside for later.\xa0 \xa0
', '
Heat a pan over medium high heat. Add in the olive oil and garlic. Sauté until the garlic turns fragrant.\xa0
', '
Then add in the wine, cherry tomatoes, butter, and Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce. Cook until the cherry tomatoes start to burst and soften.
', '
Mix in the crab meat and crushed red pepper flakes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
', '
Add the cooked linguine to the pan along with the lemon juice. Cook until the pasta is warmed through then remove the pan from heat.
', '
Serve the pasta with lemon wedges and garnish with parsley and lemon zest. Enjoy!
']

Name: citrus-glazed-salmon
Ingredients: ['1 Tbsp. olive oil', '1 orange, thinly sliced', '1 lemon, thinly sliced', '1 (2 ½ lbs.) filet of salmon', '1 Tbsp. dill fronds', 'additional dill fronds, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['salt and pepper, to taste', '½ cup Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Sauce for Orange Chicken']
Procedure:
['
Preheat the oven to 400˚F.
', '
Pour and spread the oil onto the bottom of a 9x13 inch casserole dish. Arrange some of the orange and lemon slices on the bottom of the dish, leaving a few slices to lay on top of the salmon.
', '
Place the salmon on top of the bed of citrus slices. Season the salmon with salt and pepper then brush the Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Sauce for Orange Chicken on top.\xa0
', '
Lay the remaining citrus slices on top of salmon. Sprinkle the dill fronds on top.\xa0
', '
Roast the salmon in the oven for 15-20 minutes. Garnish with additional dill fronds and enjoy!
']

Name: chicken-tarna
Ingredients: ['3 lbs. boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1 inch pieces', '6 pita bread', '2 cups shredded lettuce', '1 ½ cups halved cherry tomatoes', '1 ½ cup sliced red onion', '1 ½ cups sliced Persian cucumbers', '1 cup crumbled feta', '1 cup tzatziki sauce', 'parsley leaves, for garnish']
Marinade: ['1 cup plain Greek yogurt', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. cumin', '2 tsp. cardamom', '2 tsp. turmeric powder', '2 tsp. cinnamon', '2 tsp. kosher salt']
Procedure:
['
In a large bowl, combine the Greek yogurt, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce, cumin, cardamom, turmeric, cinnamon, and kosher salt. Add in the chicken thigh pieces and mix until the chicken is evenly coated. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and place it in the fridge to marinate for at least 1 hour.\xa0
', '
Heat the grill to a medium heat. Skewer the chicken pieces onto bamboo skewers that have been soaking in water. Grill the chicken until it is completely cooked through, then remove it from the grill.
', '
Serve the chicken with the pita bread, shredded lettuce, cherry tomatoes, red onion, Persian cucumbers, feta, and tzatziki sauce. Garnish with parsley leaves and enjoy!
']

Name: spicy-chicken-ramen
Ingredients: ['2 chicken breasts', '2 packaged ramen noodles, seasoning packets discarded', '1 soft-boiled egg, halved', 'thinly sliced radishes, for garnish', 'sliced green onions, for garnish', 'black sesame seeds, for garnish']
Marinade: ['salt and pepper, to taste', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce', '1 Tbsp. honey', '½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce', '2 garlic cloves, minced']
Sauce Mix: ['2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce', '2 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce', '2 garlic cloves, minced', '1 quart chicken stock', '2 cups water', '1-inch piece of ginger, minced', '5 oz. shiitake mushrooms, sliced', '1 Tbsp. rice vinegar']
Procedure:
['
Preheat the oven to 400˚F. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spray it with cooking spray. Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper, then place them onto the prepared baking sheet.
', '
Add 2 Tbsp. of the Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce, honey, a half Tbsp. of Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce, and half of the minced garlic to a small bowl. Mix the ingredients together then brush it onto the chicken breasts.
', '
Bake the chicken in the oven for about 20 minutes or until they are fully cooked. Remove the chicken from the oven and allow them to cool for at least 10 minutes before slicing.
', '
Add the remaining 2 Tbsp. of the Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce, remaining 2 Tbsp. of Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Premium Cooking Soy Sauce, remaining half of minced garlic, chicken stock, water, ginger, shiitake mushrooms, and rice vinegar to a pot. Mix the ingredients together and bring it to a boil.
', '
Add in the ramen noodles and cook for about 3 minutes or until the noodles are fully cooked.
', '
Portion the ramen into bowls. Add on the sliced chicken and a soft boiled egg halves. Garnish with sliced radishes, sliced green onions, and black sesame seeds. Enjoy!\xa0
']

Name: chicken-jollof
Ingredients: ['1 red onion', '2 roma tomatoes', '2 red bell peppers', '1 scotch bonnet', '1 ¼ lbs. mix of chicken thighs and drumsticks', '½ cup vegetable oil', '1 yellow onion, diced', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic ', '2 cups basmati rice', '3 dried bay leaves', '2 ½ cup hot water', 'chopped parsley, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['1 Tbsp. tomato paste', '1 tsp. white pepper', '1 tsp. dried thyme', '1 tsp. smoked paprika', '1 chicken bouillon cube']
Marinade: ['salt and pepper, to taste']
Procedure:
['
Add the red onion, tomatoes, bell peppers, and scotch bonnet to a food processor. Blend the ingredients until smooth. Heat a pan over medium heat. Add the blended ingredients to the pan and boil until the liquid has evaporated. Set it aside for later.
', '
Season both sides of the chicken thighs and drumsticks with salt and pepper. Heat a different pot over medium high heat. Add in 1 Tbsp. of vegetable oil. Sear the chicken thighs and drumsticks until golden brown on both sides then remove them from the pan.\xa0
', '
In the same pot, add in the onion and Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic. Cook until the onion turns translucent. Add in the tomato paste and cook for about 3 minutes.
', '
Mix in the white pepper, dried thyme, smoke paprika, chicken bouillon cube, and the reserved tomato and pepper puree.\xa0
', '
Add in the rice, bay leaves, and hot water. Simmer with the lid on for 25 minutes. Uncover the pot and nestle in the chicken thighs and drumsticks. Place the lid back on and cook for an additional 20 minutes.\xa0
', '
Turn off the heat and allow the rice to sit for 10 minutes. Fluff the rice and garnish with chopped parsley. Enjoy!
']

Name: seared-hoisin-tofu
Ingredients: ['16 oz. extra firm tofu, drained', '1 ½ Tbsp. vegetable oil', '1 large bunch bok choy, separated', '1 Tbsp. vegetable stock or water', '4 cups cooked white rice, for serving', 'black sesame seeds, for garnish', 'white sesame seeds, for garnish', 'sliced green onions, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['½ cup Lee Kum Kee Lime Cilantro Flavored Hoisin Sauce', 'salt and pepper to taste']
Procedure:
['
Wrap the tofu in paper towels and place it into a dish. Place a heavy object on top of the tofu to help press out any excess water. Allow the tofu to sit for 30 minutes.
', '
Unwrap the tofu and cut it in half along the long side. Slide each piece into ¼ inch thick square slices.\xa0
', '
Heat a pan over medium high heat. Add in 1 Tbsp. of the vegetable oil. Add the slices of tofu to the pan and arrange them so that they’re in a single layer. Cook the tofu on each side for about 2-3 minutes or until they turn golden brown. Add in the Lee Kum Kee Lime Cilantro Flavored Hoisin Sauce and mix until the tofu slices are evenly coated. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Set aside for later.\xa0
', '
Heat a new pan over medium heat. Add in the remaining half Tbsp. of vegetable oil. Place the bok choy into the pan. Add in the vegetable stock then cover the pan with the lid. Steam the bok choy until it has fully cooked then remove it from the pan.
', '
Serve the tofu and bok choy with a side of rice. Garnish with black and white sesame seeds and sliced green onions.
']

Name: clam-chowder
Procedure:
['
Heat a pan over medium high heat. Add the bacon to the pan and cook until crispy. Remove the bacon from the pan and place them onto a plate lined with paper towels to drain. When the bacon has cooled, roughly chop them into little bits, set aside for later.
', '
Add the celery and onion into the same pan with the bacon grease. Sauté until the vegetables are tender. Then add in the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.\xa0
', '
Add in the potatoes, Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce, water, clam juice, chicken bouillon granules, white pepper, and thyme. Bring the mixture to a boil, then lower the heat to a simmer for 15-20 minutes until the potatoes are tender.
', '
In a small bowl, mix together the flour and 1 cup of the Half & Half. Slowly pour this mixture into the soup while stirring. Bring the soup to a boil and cook until it has thickened.\xa0
', '
Mix in the remaining Half & Half and chopped clams. Ladle the soup into bowls and garnish with the chopped bacon and parsley. Enjoy!
']

Name: coconut-adobo-chicken
Ingredients: ['2 lbs. chicken drumsticks', '2 Tbsp. olive oil', '6 garlic cloves, minced', '4 dried bay leaves', 'cooked white rice, for serving', 'chopped parsley, for garnish']
Seasoning: ['ground black pepper, to taste']
Sauce Mix: ['½ cup Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce', '⅓ cup white vinegar', '¾ cup coconut milk', '¼ cup honey', '¼ cup water']
Procedure:
['
In a bowl, combine Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce, white vinegar, coconut milk, honey, and water.
', '
Season the drumsticks with the black pepper.
', '
Heat a pan over high heat. Add in the olive oil and then the drumsticks. Sear the drumsticks on all sides. Add in the garlic and cook until fragrant.\xa0
', '
Reduce the heat to low and pour in the sauce mixture along with the dried bay leaves. Cover the pan and simmer for 20 minutes.
', '
Remove the lid and cook for an additional 10-15 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked.\xa0
', '
Remove and discard the dried bay leaves. Serve the chicken with rice and garnish with chopped parsley. Enjoy!
']

Name: air-fryer-xo-fish-balls-in-honey
Ingredients: ['10 oz. fish paste']
Seasoning: ['3 ½ Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce (oil removed)', '2 Tbsp. honey']
Procedure:
['
Add Lee Kum Kee XO Sauce into the fish paste\xa0and mix well.
', '
Take a small portion (around 0.8 oz.) and knead as a ball. Repeat the step until all the mixture are done.
', '
Put the fish balls in the air-fryer. Air-fry them at 392°F for 10 minutes.
', '
Add in honey and toss well. Put it back to air-fryer again. Air-fry for 3 minutes at 356°F.
', '
Tips:
1. When cooking at home, wet your hands with water before you knead. It helps to avoid sticking your hands from the fish paste.

2. Prepare a plate with plastic wrap and brush a layer of oil. Then put the fish balls\xa0in step 2 on the plate. It helps to avoid sticking the plate from the fish balls.
']

Name: beef-and-broccoli-stir-fry
Ingredients: ['1 lb. broccoli florets', '1 lb. flank steak', '2 Tbsp. vegetable oil', '2 ea. garlic cloves, minced', '2 cups rice for serving (optional)', 'sesame seeds, for garnish (optional) ', 'cilantro, for garnish (optional) ', 'scallion, for garnish (optional)  ', 'microgreens, for garnish (optional)']
Marinade: ['1 tsp. baking soda    ', '1 tsp. cornstarch    ', '1 Tbsp. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Cooking Soy Sauce      ', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil']
Sauce Mix: ['2 oz. Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce', '2 tsp. Chinese rice wine (or white wine)', '1 tsp. Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon ', '1/3 cup water', '1 tsp. cornstarch']
Procedure:
['
PREPARE THE BROCCOLI. Blanch the broccoli in hot salted water for about 2 minutes or until slightly tender and dark green. Shock it in ice water and drain thoroughly.\xa0\xa0
', '
MARINATE THE BEEF. In a small bowl, whisk together the baking soda, cornstarch, Lee Kum Kee Cooking Soy Sauce, and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil. Pour the mixed sauces over your thinly sliced beef flank steak and let it sit for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
\xa0
', '
CREATE THE SAUCE MIX. In a separate small bowl, stir together the Lee Kum Kee Panda Oyster Sauce, Chinese rice wine, Lee Kum Kee Chicken Bouillon, water, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is fully dissolved.
', '
STIR-FRY. Heat a wok or a large skillet on high heat and add the oil. When a wisp of white smoke appears, toss the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and marinated beef into the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, continually moving the beef slices so they are brown on all sides, but still rare. Once the beef is seared, add the broccoli and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the sauce and keep it all moving.
', '
FINISH AND SERVE. Don’t be afraid to scrape any bits off the bottom of the pan before it starts to burn. Once the sauce has turned into a nice thick glaze, serve immediately with garnishes (optional). Enjoy!
']
